We inspired Mūsā, when his people appealed to him for water, to: “Strike the rock with your staff!” Twelve springs spurted out from it; every people knew their own fountain. We ˹further˺ made the clouds shade them, sent down to them the manna and quails; “Eat from the good things that We provided for you”—they did not commit injustice against Us, but they did themselves the injustice.

7:161.t: ˹Remember˺ When was said to them: “Dwell this town and eat therein whatever you like and say: “ḥiṭṭah!”; and enter through the gate prostrating ˹in thankfulness˺, and We shall forgive you your sins and We shall increase ˹the reward of˺ those who do good”.

7:162.t: The wrongdoers among them substituted what was said to them for something else, and We sent down on them a plague from the sky for their injustice.

7:163.t: And ask them ˹Muhammad˺ about the seaside town; they ˹its people˺ used to transgress on the Sabbath. Their ˹large˺ fish used to come to them forthrightly on their Sabbath, but when they were not observing the Sabbath, they ˹the fish˺ would not come to them. Thus we tested them for their contravening.

7:164.t: ˹Mention˺ When a group of them said ˹to another group˺: “Why do you admonish ˹those˺ folks whom Allah will surely destroy or punish severely?” They said: “As an apology in front of your Lord and that they might be Mindful”.

7:165.t: When they disregarded what they were reminded of, We salvaged those who forbade evil and overtook the wrongdoers with an abject punishment for their contravening.

7:166.t: When they ˹flagrantly˺ defied what they were forbidden from We said to them: “Be apes, despised!”

7:167.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ When your Lord declared ˹that˺ He would certainly send against them, until the Day of Judgement, ˹those˺ who would subject them to the worst of suffering—indeed your Lord is swift in reprisal and He certainly is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

7:168.t: And We cut them into ˹separate˺ nations ˹asunder˺ in the land; among them are those who are righteous and those who are less so. We tested them with the good and bad ˹fortune˺ so that they might return.

7:169.t: Then they were succeeded by a generation who inherited the Book; they would take the offer of that which is lower and say: “We will be forgiven!” And when ˹yet again˺ a like offer came to them, they seized it; was not the pledge of the Book taken against them that they should not say about Allah except the Truth? ˹Irrespective of that˺ They have ˹carefully˺ studied it! Indeed the Last Abode is better for the Mindful, have you no sense?

7:170.t: But those who ˹firmly˺ hold fast to the Book and keep up the Prayer, We would never render void the reward of the reformers.

7:171.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ When We uprooted the mountain ˹lifting it˺ like a canopy above them and they expected it to fall on them, ˹We said˺: “Hold fast to what We have given you and bear in mind what came into it, so that you might be Mindful”.

7:172.t: ˹Mention˺ When your Lord took the posterity of the children of Adam from their backs and made them bear witness about themselves ˹thus˺: “Am I not your Lord?” They said: “Yes, indeed! We bear witness ˹to it˺”. That, lest that you say on the Day of Judgement: “We were not aware of this!”

7:173.t: Or that you may say: “Nay, but our fathers Associated before and we were nothing but a posterity ˹taking˺ after them; do You do away with us for what falsifiers committed?”

7:174.t: Thus We expound the Signs, perchance they may return.

7:175.t: And recite to them ˹Muhammad˺ the notable news of the one to whom We gave our Signs but he skinned himself out of them, Satan ˹eagerly˺ caught up with him and he became one of the strayed ones.

7:176.t: Had We wished, We would have elevated him by ˹means of˺ them, but he stuck to the ground and followed his desire. His example is that of a dog, when you chase it away, it pants and ˹even˺ when you leave it, it pants. That is the example of the people who denied Our Signs; so recount ˹Muhammad˺ the stories perchance they may ponder.

7:177.t: Sordid is the example of the folk who denied Our Signs and did themselves a great injustice.

7:178.t: Whoever Allah guides is the truly guided, and whoever He misguides, then these are the true losers.

7:179.t: We have indeed spawned for Hellfire many among the jinn and humans; theirs are hearts with which they comprehend naught, theirs are eyes with which they see naught and theirs are ears with which they hear naught. Those are akin to livestock, nay but they are more at loss; these are the unmindful.

7:180.t: To Allah belong the Beautiful Names, so pray to Him by them and leave out those who skew His Names; they will be requited for what they used to do.

7:181.t: And among those whom We have created, is a nation who guide with the Truth and with it they administer justice.

7:182.t: But those who denied Our Signs, We will lure them ˹gradually˺ whereof they know not;

7:183.t: I will allow them time—verily My Machination is robust.

7:184.t: Did they not perceive that their fellow has no madness in him? He is none but a fluent warner!

7:185.t: Did they not look at the dominion of the Heavens and Earth, and whatever thing that Allah created; perchance that their end has drawn near—in what speech else besides it do they ˹then˺ believe?

7:186.t: Whoever Allah sends astray, there is no guide for him; He leaves them to wander aimlessly in their blindness.

7:187.t: They ask you ˹Muhammad˺ about the Hour: “When is its materialization?” Say: “This knowledge is with none but my Lord. None reveals it at its ˹due˺ time except Him. Heavy it is in the Heavens and on Earth. It will come to you naught but all of a sudden”. They ask you ˹Muhammad˺ as if you were too keen on it! Say: “Its knowledge is with none but Allah”—indeed most people know ˹that˺ not.

7:188.t: Say: “I have no power over benefit or harm coming my way, except ˹it being˺ what Allah willed. Had I ˹any˺ knowledge of the Unseen, I would have stockpiled ˹all that is˺ good and no harm would have touched me. I am none but a warner and a bearer of glad tidings to those who Believe”.

7:189.t: He is the One Who created you from a single soul and from it He made its mate so that he may find assuagement in her. When he pulled himself over her, she became lightly pregnant, and she ˹carefreely˺ spent it ˹her pregnancy˺; when it became heavy, they both prayed to Allah, their Lord: “Should You grant us a well one, we shall surely be among the grateful”.

7:190.t: When He granted them both a well one, they claimed Associates with Him in what He granted them both—exalted be Allah above ˹all˺ what they Associate ˹with Him˺.

7:191.t: Do they Associate ˹with Him˺ that which would not create, while they ˹themselves˺ are being created?

7:192.t: They are not able to avail them with help, nor are they able of helping themselves.

7:193.t: If you call them to guidance, they would not follow you; it is all the same whether you call them or you were to remain silent.

7:194.t: Verily those whom you invoke besides Allah are naught but subjects like you; invoke them then and let them respond to you if only you were truthful.

7:195.t: Do they have legs with which they walk? Or do they have hands with which they smite? Or do they have eyes with which they see? Or do they have ears with which they hear? Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Round up your Associates and conspire against me without delay;

7:196.t: Verily my Ally is Allah, Who sent down the Book; He covers the righteous in ˹His˺ Alliance;

7:197.t: but those whom you invoke besides Him, they cannot avail you with help nor are they able of helping themselves”.

7:198.t: If you call them for guidance, they could not hear, and you ˹Muhammad˺ see them look at you but they do not see.

7:199.t: Accept ˹Muhammad˺ whatever is bearable, enjoin what is acceptable and turn away from the ignorant.

7:200.t: Should Satan somehow stir you ˹Muhammad˺, seek refuge with Allah—indeed He is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

7:201.t: Indeed those who are Mindful when touched by a rounder visitation of Satan’s, they remember and lo they become clear-sighted.

7:202.t: But their brothers, they ˹the devils˺ pave a long path for them in error and they do not hold back.

7:203.t: If you ˹Muhammad˺ do not bring them a Sign, they would say: “Would you not just come up with it?” Say: “I merely follow what is being revealed to me from my Lord. This ˹Qur’an˺ is ˹a plethora of˺ eye-openers from your Lord, a guidance and mercy to those who Believe”.

7:204.t: When the Qur’an is recited, listen to it ˹people˺ and hearken, so that you might be shown mercy.

7:205.t: And mention your Lord to yourself, humbly and fearfully; lower than audible speech, early and late in the day, and be not among the unmindful.

7:206.t: Indeed those who are with your Lord do not disdain from His worship, they glorify Him and to Him they prostrate ۩.

8:1.t: They ask you ˹Muhammad˺ about the spoils, say: “The spoils are for Allah and the Messenger. So be ˹truly˺ Mindful of Allah, mend your bond ˹of brotherhood˺ and obey Allah and His Messenger, if only you are ˹truly˺ Believers”.

8:2.t: Indeed the ˹true˺ Believers are the ones who when Allah is mentioned their hearts tremble with awe, when His Signs are recited for them their Faith increases and they ˹devoutly˺ put their trust in their Lord;

8:3.t: the ones who keep up the Prayer and ˹charitably˺ spend out from what We provided them with.

8:4.t: Those are truly the Believers; they have ˹elevated˺ grades with their Lord, forgiveness and generous provision.

8:5.t: That is like your Lord’s bringing you out of your home with the Truth, ˹while˺ indeed a party of the Believers were reluctant;

8:6.t: they argue with you regarding the Truth after it has become evident, as if they were being driven to death while staring ˹at it˺.

8:7.t: ˹Remember˺ When Allah promised you ˹either˺ one of the two ˹enemy˺ sections to be yours; you wished the one without the thorn to be yours, ˹but˺ Allah wanted to confirm the Truth with His Words, and utterly wipe out the Deniers;

8:8.t: to establish the Truth and abolish falsehood, even to the acrimony of the criminals.

8:9.t: ˹Remember˺ When you ˹Believers˺ implored your Lord for assistance and He responded to you that: “I am reinforcing you with one thousand angels, following ˹one another˺ closely on their heels”.

8:10.t: Allah made this none but a glad harbinger and that your hearts may become comforted with it; ˹but˺ victory comes from none but Allah – Allah is verily All-Prevailing, All-Wise.

8:11.t: ˹Remember˺When He enwrapped you with sleepiness as assurance from Him and sent down on you water from the sky to purify you with it, dispel the taint of Satan from you, brace your hearts and make firm with it ˹your˺ feet.

8:12.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ When your Lord inspired the angels: “I am indeed with you so make firm those who Believe. I shall cast fright in the hearts of those who Deny; smite then above the necks and smite every fingertip of theirs”.

8:13.t: That for pitting themselves against Allah and His Messenger; whoever pits himself against Allah and His Messenger, then Allah is severe in punishment.

8:14.t: That ˹is your punishment˺, so taste it then; indeed the Deniers’ is the Punishment of Hellfire.

8:15.t: You who Believe, when you meet the Deniers marshalled ˹in hosts˺ do not show them your hinds.

8:16.t: Whoever shows them his hind on that day – except one who turns round to fight back, or to align ˹himself˺ with ˹another˺ company – then he goes back with Wrath from Allah and his resort is Hellfire; awful indeed is the destination.

8:17.t: You ˹Believers˺ did not kill them, but Allah killed them; you ˹Muhammad˺ did not cast but Allah did the casting; to have the Believers do well by His Grace – verily Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

8:18.t: ˹All˺ That and ˹in addition˺ Allah will surely dull the plotting of the Deniers.

8:19.t: If you ˹Deniers˺ seek ˹Allah’s˺ arbitration, then the arbitration has come upon you; but if you desist, it would be better for you; if you come back We shall come back. Your host will avail you nothing, numerous as it might be. And ˹know˺ that Allah is surely with the Believers.

8:20.t: You who Believe, obey Allah and His Messenger and do not turn away from him while you hear ˹him˺.

8:21.t: Do not be like the ones who said: “We are listening”, while they listen not.

8:22.t: Indeed the worst of all the creatures with Allah are the deaf and dumb – those who cannot heed.

8:23.t: Had Allah known any good in them, He would have made them listen; ˹even˺ had He made them listen, they would have turned away, looking the other way.

8:24.t: You who Believe, respond to Allah and the Messenger when he calls you to what gives you life; know that Allah may intervene between a man and his heart and that to Him you shall be gathered.

8:25.t: Beware of a trial that will befall not only the wrongdoers among you and know that Allah is severe in punishment.

8:26.t: Remember when you were only but few, victimized in the land, fearing that people will snatch you away; He then gave you a haven, aided you with His victory and blessed you with good provisions, so that you may be thankful.

8:27.t: You who Believe, do not betray Allah and the Messenger, and ˹do not˺ betray your trusts knowingly.

8:28.t: Know that your money and children are but a trial and that with Allah is a great reward.

8:29.t: You who Believe, if you are Mindful of Allah, He will grant you a distinguisher, absolve you of your foul deeds and forgive you—Allah is indeed of great favour.

8:30.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ When the Deniers plotted against you to seize you, kill you or expel you; they plot and Allah plans—Allah is verily the best of planners.

8:31.t: And when Our Signs are recited to them, they say: “We have listened! Had we wished, we would have come up with something like this; this is nothing but merely ˹a retelling of the˺ scribbles of the ancients!”

8:32.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ When they ˹the Deniers˺ said: “O Allah, if this is the Truth from You, then rain on us stones from the sky or bring us a painful punishment!”

8:33.t: Allah would not have punished them while you ˹Muhammad˺ were ˹still˺ among them; Allah would not punish them while they ˹sincerely˺ seek forgiveness.

8:34.t: But why would not Allah punish them while they turn ˹people˺ away from the Sanctified Mosque; ˹although˺ they were not its ˹rightful˺ patrons—its ˹true˺ patrons are none but the Mindful, but most of them know ˹that˺ not.

8:35.t: Their Prayer at the House was nothing but ˹hollow˺ whistling and clapping; taste then the punishment for your ˹adamant˺ Denying.

8:36.t: Verily those who Deny do spend their money to turn ˹people˺ away from the path of Allah; they will spend it, then it will be ˹a cause of˺ ˹bitter˺ regret for them, and then they will ˹eventually˺ be overpowered—those who Deny will be gathered to Hellfire.

8:37.t: ˹This is˺ For Allah to set the evil apart from the good; He will mount the evil on top of each other making it into a heap and consign it all to Hellfire—these are the ˹real˺ losers.

8:38.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺ to those who Deny, if they desist they will be forgiven ˹all˺ what went on before, and if they return then the canon of the ancients already came to pass.

8:39.t: Fight them until there is no ˹more˺ trial and religion becomes wholly Allah’s. But if they desist, then Allah is surely All-Seeing of what they do.

8:40.t: But if they turn away, then know ˹Believers˺ that Allah is your Ally; most honorable is the Ally, most honorable is the Supporter.

8:41.t: And know ˹Believers˺ that whatever gain you make a fifth of it is for Allah and the Messenger, the kith and kin, the orphans, the needy, the destitute and the stranded; ˹that is˺ if you have truly Believed in Allah and what We sent down to Our Servant on the Distinguisher Day when the two hosts met—verily Allah is Able over everything.

8:42.t: When you were at the nearer bank ˹of the valley˺ and they were at the further bank, and the mounters were lower than you are; had you made a mutual appointment, you would have ˹both˺ missed it, but ˹that˺ so Allah would bring to pass a foregone affair. So that whoever perishes, perishes upon clear evidence and whoever lives, lives with clear evidence—verily Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

8:43.t: ˹Remember˺ When Allah showed them to you ˹Muhammad˺ few ˹in numbers˺ in your sleep, had He showed them numerous to you, you ˹Believers˺ would have faltered and disagreed over the matter, but Allah saved ˹the day˺—He is All-Knowing of what the chests conceal.

8:44.t: ˹Remember˺ When He showed them to you ˹Believers˺ few in your eyes, when you met ˹each other˺, and He showed you few in their ˹the Deniers˺ eyes, so that He would bring to pass a forgone affair—to Allah all matters are returned.

8:45.t: You who Believe, when you meet a force ˹in battle˺ stand your ground and mention Allah abundantly, may you succeed.

8:46.t: And Obey Allah and His Messenger, do not disagree then you would falter and lose your wind and be steadfast—indeed Allah is with those who are steadfast.

8:47.t: Do not be like the ones who set out from the homelands, full of conceit and putting on an act for people, turning ˹people˺ away from the path of Allah—Allah is encompassing of what they do.

8:48.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ When Satan prettified their deeds for them and said: “None of all people can overcome you this day” and “I am a deliverer of yours!” When the two hosts gazed upon each other, he turned on his heels and said: “I disown you! I see that which you do not! I fear Allah; Allah is severe in punishment!”

8:49.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is disease said: “These are surely hoodwinked by their religion!” But whoever puts his trust in Allah, then Allah is All-Prevailing, All-Wise.

8:50.t: If only you could see ˹Muhammad˺ when the angels take the lives of the Deniers beating their faces and their backsides, and ˹say to them˺: “Taste you the Punishment of burning!”

8:51.t: “That for what your hands have sent forth, verily Allah never deals ˹His˺ servants unjustly.”

8:52.t: ˹Just˺ like the habitual ways of the people of Pharaoh and those ˹who came˺ before them; they Denied the Signs of Allah and Allah seized them ˹in Punishment˺ for their sins—indeed Allah is All-Powerful, severe in Punishment.

8:53.t: That for Allah would never alter a favour He bestows on a ˹certain˺ people until they alter what is in themselves—verily Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

8:54.t: ˹Just˺ like the habitual ways of the people of Pharaoh and those ˹who came˺ before them; they rejected the Signs of their Lord and We destroyed them for their sins and We drowned the people of Pharaoh; they all were unjust.

8:55.t: Indeed the worst of all the creatures with Allah are the ones who Denied and never do they Believe.

8:56.t: Among them are the ones with whom you ˹Muhammad˺ forged ˹peace˺ pacts, then they break their pact every time; never are they Mindful.

8:57.t: Whenever you encounter them in war set them as a fearsome example to those behind them, may they reflect.

8:58.t: Whenever you ˹Muhammad˺ fear treachery from certain people, then cast ˹their pact˺ to them ˹so that all are˺ on level ground—indeed Allah Loves not the treacherous.

8:59.t: Let not the Deniers think they slipped away; they can never frustrate ˹Me˺.

8:60.t: Prepare for them whatever you can of force and steeds tethered, to overawe with it Allah’s enemy and your enemy, and others besides them that you know not; Allah Knows them.Whatever it is that you spend in the path of Allah will be paid back to you in full and you shall not be wronged.

8:61.t: If they incline towards peace, incline towards it ˹Muhammad˺ and put your trust in Allah—verily He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

8:62.t: If they intend to trick you, then Allah is certainly sufficient for you; He is the One Who braced you with His Help and with the Believers.

8:63.t: He also made their hearts join ˹harmoniously˺; had you ˹Muhammad˺ spent all that is in the Earth, you would not have joined their hearts. But Allah joined them—verily He is All-Prevailing, All-Wise.

8:64.t: O Prophet, Allah is sufficient for you and for the Believers who follow you.

8:65.t: O Prophet, rouse the Believers to fight;if there are twenty of you who are steadfast, they will overcome two hundred and if there are a hundred of you ˹who are steadfast˺, they will overcome one thousand of the Deniers for their being people who grasp not.

8:66.t: Now, Allah lightened ˹your load˺ off of you ˹for˺ He Knows that there is weakness in you; if there are a hundred of you who are steadfast, they will overcome two hundred and if there are a thousand of you, they will overcome two thousand with Allah’s permission—Allah is indeed with the steadfast.

8:67.t: No Prophet should have held captives until he strikes mighty in the land. You ˹Believers˺ want the goods of the worldly life and Allah wants the Hereafter—Allah is indeed All-Prevailing, All-Wise.

8:68.t: Had it not been for a decree that had been passed, a severe punishment would have touched you for what you took.

8:69.t: Eat then of what you gained, that is lawful and good, and be Mindful of Allah—verily Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

8:70.t: O Prophet, say to the captives under your hands: “Should Allah Know ˹any˺ good in your hearts, He will grant you better than what was taken from you, and forgive you—Allah is indeed All-Forgiving, Most Merciful”.

8:71.t: But if they intend to betray you ˹Muhammad˺ then surely they had betrayed Allah in the aforetime and He got ˹you˺ the better of them—Allah is indeed All-Knowing, All-Wise.

8:72.t: Verily those who have Believed, migrated and strove in the path of Allah with their money and their lives, and the ones who welcomed in their fold and supported; these are the allies of one another. And those who have Believed but did not migrate, their patronage is not onerous on you in the least until they migrate. But if they seek your support over ˹a matter of˺ religion then you have to come to their help unless against some people with whom you have a binding agreement—Allah is indeed All-Seeing of what you do.

8:73.t: Those who Deny are allies of one another; but if you ˹Believers˺ do not abide by it, there will be trial in the land and great corruption.

8:74.t: Those who have Believed, migrated and strove in the path of Allah, and the ones who welcomed in their fold and supported; these are the true Believers, theirs is forgiveness and generous provision.

8:75.t: Those ˹who have˺ Believed afterwards, migrated and strove with you ˹Believers˺ are also of your own; and close relatives have more of a right to each other ˹as˺ in the Book of Allah—verily Allah has knowledge of everything.

9:1.t: ˹This is˺ A repudiation ˹declared˺ by Allah and His Messenger to those of the Associators with whom you have forged pacts.

9:2.t: Roam around then ˹you Associators˺ in the land for four months and know that you shall not frustrate Allah and that Allah shall disgrace the Deniers.

9:3.t: And a proclamation from Allah and His Messenger on the greater day of Pilgrimage that Allah is disassociated from the Associators and His Messenger ˹is disassociated from them too˺. But if you repent then it is better for you and if you turn away then know that you shall not frustrate Allah; give tidings to those who Deny of a painful punishment.

9:4.t: Except those with whom you have forged pacts and have not failed you in anything ˹agreed˺ nor lent support to anyone against you, then observe their pact for them until the end of its term—verily Allah loves the Mindful.

9:5.t: When the sacrosanct months are drawn away, kill then the Associators wherever you find them, seize them and enclose them, and lie for them in wait in every lookout. But if they repent, keep up the Prayer and give out the prescribed alms then let them go their way—verily Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

9:6.t: Should any of the Associators seek your protection ˹Muhammad˺, then grant him protection, may he listen to the words of Allah, then deliver him to a place of safety, that for them being people who know not.

9:7.t: How can there be a pact for the Associators with Allah and with His Messenger! Except those with whom you have forged a pact at the Sanctified Mosque; as long as they remain upright to you ˹Believers˺, then remain upright to them—verily Allah loves the Mindful.

9:8.t: How ˹can it be˺, when if they were to have the upper hand over you, they would have no respect for either kinship or oaths concerning you! They placate you with their mouths, but their hearts ˹vehemently˺ refuse; indeed most of them are transgressors.

9:9.t: They traded off the Signs of Allah for a pittance and hence turned ˹people˺ away from His path—sordid indeed what they used to ˹persistently˺ commit.

9:10.t: They respect neither kinship nor oaths concerning any Believer; these are the real aggressors!

9:11.t: But if they repent, keep up the Prayer and give out the prescribed alms then ˹they are˺ your brothers in religion; We expound the Signs for people who know.

9:12.t: But if they revoke their oaths hence ˹taken˺ and slander your religion, then fight the heads of Denial – indeed they honour not their ˹binding˺ oaths – so that they may desist.

9:13.t: Would you not fight ˹those˺ folks who revoked their oaths, were intent on expelling the Messenger; being the ones who started it off against you first! Do you fear them? Then Allah is more worthy that you should fear Him—that is if only you were ˹truly˺ Believers.

9:14.t: Fight them; Allah will make them suffer at your hands, disgrace them, grant you victory over them, heal the hearts of ˹some˺ Believing folks,

9:15.t: and dispel the bitterness of their hearts. Allah relents on whoever He wishes—Allah is indeed All-Knowing, All-Wise.

9:16.t: Or you ˹Believers˺ thought that you would be left ˹alone˺ ere Allah Knows those of you who strove and did not take any, besides Allah, nor the Messenger, nor the Believers as bosom friends—Allah is indeed Knowledgeable of whatever you do.

9:17.t: It is not for the Associators to ˹devoutly˺ tend Allah’s places of prostration, while they bear witness against themselves of Denial. These, their deeds will come to nothing and in Hellfire they abide eternally.

9:18.t: None ˹truly devoutly˺ tends Allah’s places of prostration except who Believes in Allah, the Last Day, keeps up the Prayer, gives out the prescribed alms and fears none but Allah; may these very well be among the guided.

9:19.t: Do you ˹people˺ deem alike ˹your˺ providing water for pilgrims and ˹outwardly˺ tending the Sanctified Mosque, and who Believes in Allah and the Last Day and strives in Allah’s path! They are never equal before Allah—Allah never guides the unjust people.

9:20.t: Verily those who have Believed, migrated and striven in the path of Allah with their money and their lives are greater in rank before Allah—these are the ˹true˺ winners.

9:21.t: Allah gives them the glad tidings of a Mercy from Him, Pleasure and Gardens in which theirs is everlasting bliss.

9:22.t: They last therein forever—verily with Allah lies a great reward.

9:23.t: You who Believe, do not hold your fathers and brothers as allies should they prefer Denial over Belief. Whoever of you takes them as allies, then these are the ˹real˺ wrongdoers.

9:24.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “If your fathers, children, brothers, spouses, clans, ˹hard˺ earned wealth, a trade that you fear decline for and abodes you are pleased with are dearer to you than Allah, His Messenger and striving in His path, then lie in wait until Allah brings His command ˹to pass˺—Allah never guides the transgressing people.

9:25.t: Indeed Allah granted you victory in many a field, but on the day of Ḥunayn as you gloated over your multitude, it availed you nothing and the land tightened around you despite its vastness and you fled turning on your heels.

9:26.t: Then Allah sent down His tranquility on His Messenger and on the Believers. And He sent down soldiers that you did not see; He tormented the Deniers—that is the ˹just˺ requital of the Deniers.

9:27.t: Then after that Allah relents on whoever He wishes; Allah is indeed All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

9:28.t: You who Believe, the Associators are but impure so let them not come near the Sanctified Mosque after this year of theirs. If you fear want, then Allah will enrich you with His bounty, should He will—verily Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

9:29.t: Fight those who do not Believe in Allah and the Last Day, neither do they deem forbidden what Allah and His Messenger forbade and do not abide by the religion of Truth among those who were given the Book until they hand in the jizyah ˹tax˺ while humbled.

9:30.t: The Jews said: “ʿUzayr is the son of Allah!” and the Christians said: “The Messiah is the son of Allah!” That is what they say with their mouths, emulating ˹thus˺ the saying of those who Denied before; Allah damns them, how far do they avert ˹from the Truth˺!

9:31.t: They took their rabbis and their monks as Lords besides Allah – as well as the Messiah, son of Maryam – while they were not commanded except to worship One God; there is no god but Him—exalted be He over what they Associate ˹with Him˺.

9:32.t: They want to extinguish the Light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah refuses ˹all˺ but only to perfect His Light; even to the acrimony of the Deniers.

9:33.t: He is Who sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of Truth to make it outshine all ˹other˺ religions; even to the acrimony of the Associators.

9:34.t: You who Believe, many of the rabbis and monks devour people’s money in falsehood and turn ˹others˺ away from the path of Allah.Those who hoard gold and silver and do not spend them in the cause of Allah give them ˹Muhammad˺ the tidings of a painful Punishment.

9:35.t: On the Day when they are heated up in Hellfire and seared with them will be their foreheads, their sides and their backs: “This is what you hoarded for yourselves; taste then what you used to hoard!”

9:36.t: Verily the number of months with Allah is twelve months – ˹so ordained˺ in the Book of Allah ˹from the time˺ when He created the Heavens and Earth – four of which are sacrosanct. That is the upright religion; so do not wrong yourselves during them.Fight the Associators as a whole as they fight you as a whole and know that Allah is with the Mindful.

9:37.t: Indeed ˹practicing˺ the nasī’ ˹deferral˺ is nothing but an increase in Denial with which those who Deny are mislead; they deem it lawful one year and unlawful another year so that they match the number of what Allah made sacrosanct. Prettified for them was made the wickedness of their deeds; Allah guides not the ˹ardently˺ Denying people.

9:38.t: You who Believe, what is it with you when it is said to you: “Mobilize in the path of Allah!” you sluggishly move, clinging heavily to the ground? Are you satisfied with the worldly life rather than the Hereafter? How ˹very˺ little is the enjoyment of the worldly life ˹compared to that˺ in the Hereafter.

9:39.t: Should you not mobilize, He will inflict a severe punishment on you and replace another people in your stead, and you will not harm Him in the least—Allah is indeed Able over everything.

9:40.t: Should you not aid him ˹The Messenger (ﷺ)˺, then Allah had aided him, when those who Denied expelled him, ˹as he was˺ one of two in the ˹small˺ cave when he said to his Companion: “Grieve not, Allah is verily with us”. Then Allah sent down His tranquillity on him, braced him with soldiers that you did not see and ˹thus˺ made the word of those who Deny the lower one; and the Word of Allah is ˹ever˺ the higher—verily Allah is All-Prevailing, All-Wise.

9:41.t: Mobilize, light and heavy, and strive with your money and lives in the path of Allah; that is better for you if ˹only˺ you know.

9:42.t: Had it been a handy ˹worldly˺ gain or a convenient travel, they would have followed you but far away was the ˹gruelling˺ journey for them. They will swear ˹to you˺ by Allah: “Were we able, we would have set out with you!” They destroy themselves! Allah Knows that they are indeed liars.

9:43.t: May Allah forgive you ˹Muhammad˺, why did you grant them permission ˹to stay behind˺! ˹You should have not˺ Until it became clear to you those who are truthful and you know the liars.

9:44.t: Those who Believe in Allah and the Last Day would not seek your permission ˹Muhammad˺ not to strive with their money and lives—Allah is All-Knowing of the Mindful.

9:45.t: None will seek your permission but those who do not Believe in Allah and the Last Day and their hearts are suspicious; so they are ˹perpetually˺ fluctuating, ˹immersed˺ in their suspicion.

9:46.t: Had they ˹really˺ meant to set out, they would have prepared for it with ˹proper˺ preparation. But Allah was averse to their arising and put a damper on them and it was said ˹to them˺: “Sit with those who are sitting!”

9:47.t: Had they set out among you, they would have increased you nothing but quandary and they would have hurried about in your midst; they wish you ˹nothing but turbulent˺ trial and among you are those who ˹are prone to˺ lend them their ears—Allah is indeed All-Knowing of the wrongdoers.

9:48.t: They had meant you ˹Believers, turbulent˺ trial before and turned matters on all sides ˹hatching plots˺ against you ˹Muhammad˺ until the Truth came and Allah’s command prevailed to their acrimony.

9:49.t: Among them are those who say: “Grant me permission and do not expose me to trial!” Nay, but into trial they have ˹really˺ fallen! Hell is verily surrounding the Deniers.

9:50.t: Should a good thing come your way ˹Muhammad˺, they become saddened by it, if a calamity befalls you, they would say: “We have already taken our precautions!” and they turn away elated.

9:51.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Nothing will befall us except what Allah decreed for us; He is our Ally!” Let the Believers put their trust in Allah.

9:52.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Do you ˹expectantly˺ await for us nothing but one of the two good things! But what we ˹expectantly˺ await for you is that Allah afflicts you with a punishment from His side, or at our hands! So await, for we surely are awaiting with you!”

9:53.t: Say: “Spend willingly or grudgingly; it will not be accepted from you for your being contravening people!”

9:54.t: What prevented their expending being accepted from them was nothing but their Denial in Allah and His Messenger; they only come to Prayers being sluggish and only spend being grudging.

9:55.t: Let not ˹Muhammad˺ their wealth nor their sons amuse you; Allah wants nothing but to punish them with these in the worldly life and their souls to expire while they are ˹still˺ Deniers.

9:56.t: They swear by Allah that they are surely ˹part˺ of you but they are not ˹part˺ of you, ˹in reality˺ they are ˹ever˺ terrified folks;

9:57.t: should they find ˹themselves just˺ any hideout, a cavity or a bolthole, they would have darted to it galloping.

9:58.t: Among them some who snide at you regarding ˹the prescribed˺ alms; if given from it, they become contented, but if they are not given from it, they no sooner become spiteful.

9:59.t: If only they contented themselves with what Allah and His Messenger had given them, and said: “Allah is sufficient for us; Allah – and His Messenger – will provide for us from His Bounty. We ˹only˺ turn to our Lord ˹in hope˺”.

9:60.t: ˹The prescribed˺ Alms are but for the destitutes, the needy, those who work on them, the heart-attracted, by way of ˹freeing˺ necks, the ˹deeply˺ indebted, in the path of Allah and the stranded; an obligation ordained by Allah—Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

9:61.t: Among them are those who hurt the Prophet and say: “He is an ear ˹credulous˺!” Say: “‘An ear’ for your best! He Believes in Allah and believes the Believers; a mercy to those who have ˹truly˺ Believed among you.” Those who hurt the Messenger of Allah, theirs is a painful Punishment.

9:62.t: They swear by Allah to you ˹Believers˺ to reconcile with you, but Allah and His Messenger have more right to reconcile with, if ˹only˺ they were Believers.

9:63.t: Did they not know that whoever takes a stand against Allah and His Messenger, his is Hellfire forever he abides therein—that is the great disgrace!

9:64.t: The hypocrites dread that a sura will be brought down on them, revealing to them what is ˹hidden˺ in their hearts; say: “Keep on mocking; verily Allah will expose what you dread”.

9:65.t: Should you ˹Muhammad˺ ask them, they will say: “We were just ˹idly˺ chit-chatting and passing time!” Say: “Were you mocking of Allah, His Signs and His Messenger!”

9:66.t: “Do not apologize! You have truly Denied after your ˹outwardly˺ Belief! Should We pardon a bunch of you, We would ˹conversely˺ punish another bunch for being staunchly criminal!”

9:67.t: The hypocrites, both male and female, take after each other; they enjoin what is unacceptable and advise against virtue, and clinch their fists. They forgot Allah and He Forgot them—verily the hypocrites are the contravening ones.

9:68.t: Allah promises the hypocrites, male and female, and the Deniers Hellfire, forever they abide therein; it is sufficient for them and that Allah Damns them—theirs ˹therein˺ is resident Punishment.”

9:69.t: Just like the ones before them: “They were mightier in strength than you are and greater in wealth and children. They enjoyed their ˹worldly˺ portion and you enjoyed your portion as much as the ones before you enjoyed their portion, and you indulged as much as they indulged; these their deeds were nullified in this worldly life and in the Hereafter—these are ˹the real˺ losers.

9:70.t: Had not the news of those who came before them reached them: the people of Nūḥ, ˹the tribes of˺ ʿĀd and Thamūd, the people of Ibrāhīm, the companions of Midian and the overturned; their Messengers came to them with clear evidences! Allah would not have wronged them but they were bent on wronging themselves.

9:71.t: And Believers, male and female, are ˹mutually˺ each other’s allies. They enjoin virtue and advise against what is unacceptable, keep up the Prayer and give out the prescribed alms, and obey Allah and His Messenger; those Allah will have mercy on them—verily Allah is All-Prevailing, All-Wise.

9:72.t: Allah promised the Believers, male and female, Gardens under which rivers flow forever they abide therein and comely abodes in Gardens of Eternity, but a Pleasure from Allah is ˹ever˺ greater; that is ˹truly˺ the great triumph.

9:73.t: O Prophet, strive against the Deniers and the hypocrites and be tough on them, their resort is Hellfire; dire indeed is the end.

9:74.t: They swear ˹to you˺ that they did not utter ˹the word of Denial˺; surely they have uttered the word of Denial so they Denied after their ˹professing˺ Islam. They resolved ˹to carry out˺ what they failed to attain; they only bore grudges after Allah – and His Messengers – enriched them with His bounty! If they repent, it would be better for them but if they turn away, Allah will inflict a painful punishment on them ˹both˺ in the worldly life and in the Hereafter—˹and then˺ they will have no ally or helper.

9:75.t: Among them are those who pledged to Allah: “Should He bestow on us from His bounty, we would ˹commit to˺ spend charitably and ˹commit to˺ be among the righteous”.

9:76.t: But when He bestowed on them from His bounty, they became miserly with it, and ˹wilfully˺ turned away paying no heed.

9:77.t: Then there pursued them hypocrisy in their hearts until the Day they meet Him; for reneging on what they pledged to Allah and for their persistent lying.

9:78.t: Did they not know that Allah Knows their secrets and their private conversations, and that Allah is ever All-Knower of all the unseen!

9:79.t: Those who snide at the ˹generously˺ benefacting among the Believers regarding alms, as well as ˹the sincere˺ who can find nothing ˹to give˺ but their own effort – they ridicule them – may Allah Ridicule them and theirs is a painful Punishment.

9:80.t: Ask ˹Muhammad˺ for forgiveness for them, or do not ask for forgiveness for them; should you ask for forgiveness for them seventy times, Allah will not forgive them. That for their Denying of Allah and His Messenger; Allah guides not the ˹persistently˺ contravening people.

9:81.t: The stay-backers were pleased with their station behind the Messenger of Allah; they hated to strive in the path of Allah with their money and lives and said: “Do not mobilize in the heat!” Say ˹Muhammad˺: “The heat of Hellfire is surely more severe” – if only they would discern.

9:82.t: Let them laugh a little but weep much they shall; a ˹just˺ requital for what they used to earn.

9:83.t: Should Allah return you back ˹Muhammad˺ to a band of them and they sought your permission to set out, say: “You shall never set out with me ever and shall never fight an enemy with me. You were satisfied to stay back in the first time! Stay then with the fall-behinders!”

9:84.t: Do not ever Pray ˹Muhammad˺ over anyone of them who dies and do not stand at his grave; they had Denied Allah and His Messenger and died while in a state of contravention.

9:85.t: Let not ˹Muhammad˺ their wealth and their sons amuse you; Allah wants nothing but to punish them with these in the worldly life and their souls to expire while they are ˹still˺ Deniers.

9:86.t: When a sura is brought down ˹commanding people˺ to Believe in Allah and strive with His Messenger, the well-off among them seek your permission and say: “Leave us to be with those who sit ˹at home˺!”

9:87.t: They were content to be among the ˹incapacitated˺ stayers and Allah stamped on their hearts so that they would not discern.

9:88.t: But the Messenger and Believers alongside him strove with their money and lives; those are the ones for whom is all good ˹things˺ and those are the winners.

9:89.t: Allah readied for them Gardens under which rivers flow forever they abide therein; that is ˹truly˺ the great triumph.

9:90.t: And the permission seekers among the herds people came to be pardoned, but those who lied to Allah and His Messenger stayed; a painful Punishment shall befall those who Denied among them.

9:91.t: There is no guilt upon the weak, or the sick, or those who cannot find the means to spend, should they be honest to Allah and His Messenger; there is no way against the good-doers—Allah is indeed All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

9:92.t: Neither is there ˹a way˺ against those who when they come to you to mount them ˹on riding animals˺ – you ˹Muhammad˺ would say: “I cannot secure what to mount you on” – they turn back with their eyes swelling with tears, deeply sorrowful that they cannot find the means to spend.

9:93.t: The way is only ˹rightly sought˺ against those who seek your permission while they are well-to-do; they were content to be among the ˹incapacitated˺ stayers and Allah stamped on their hearts so that they would not know.

9:94.t: They apologize to you ˹Believers˺ when you return to them. Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Do not apologize. We will not believe you; Allah has told us some of your news. Allah – and His Messenger – shall see your deeds, then you shall be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed and He will break the news to you about what you used to do!”

9:95.t: They will swear by Allah to you, when you come back to them so that you would turn away from them. Turn away from them then; they are filthy and their resort is Hell; a ˹just˺ requital for what they used to earn.

9:96.t: They swear to you so that you may reconcile with them. If you reconcile with them then Allah does not reconcile with the contravening people.

9:97.t: Herds people are stauncher in Denial and hypocrisy; they are more likely not to know the boundaries of what Allah sent down to His Messenger—Allah is indeed All-Knowing, All-Wise.

9:98.t: Among the herds people are those who take what they spend to be a liability and lie in wait for turns of fortune against you ˹Believers˺; may the unfortunate turn be against them! Allah is indeed All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

9:99.t: Yet among the herds people are those who Believe in Allah and the Last Day and take what they spend to be reparation with Allah and ˹to seek˺ the Messenger’s prayers ˹for them˺; indubitably it is a reparation for them! Allah will admit them into His Mercy—Allah is verily All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

9:100.t: The earliest forerunners among the Immigrants and the Helpers, and those who followed their suit in perfection ˹of deeds˺, Allah is Pleased with them and they are pleased with Him, and He has readied for them Gardens under which rivers flow forever after they abide therein; that is ˹truly˺ the great triumph.

9:101.t: Among the herds people ˹who live˺ around you are ˹yet˺ hypocrites and ˹yet˺ among the people of Madinah are those who are mired in hypocrisy. You ˹Muhammad˺ do not know them, ˹but˺ We Know them; We shall punish them twice, then they shall be returned to a grave Punishment.

9:102.t: And ˹there are˺ others who admitted their guilt; they mixed a good deed with another ˹which is˺ bad. Allah may very well relent towards them—Allah is verily All-Forgiving, Most-Merciful.

9:103.t: Take from their money alms with which you purify and elevate them, and pray for them; verily your prayers are restfulness for them—Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

9:104.t: Did they not know that Allah is the One Who accepts repentance from His servants and takes ˹their˺ alms, and that Allah is the One Who is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful!

9:105.t: Say: “Do your deeds! Allah shall see your deeds, as well as His Messenger and the Believers. And you shall be returned to the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed and He will break the news to you about what you used to do!

9:106.t: Others ˹among them˺ are postponed until ˹the coming of˺ Allah’s command; He either punishes them or relents on them—Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

9:107.t: And ˹among them are˺ those who assumed ˹for themselves˺ a mosque to cause damage, in Denial, to cause discord among the Believers and in anticipation of he who had fought Allah and His Messenger before. They will surely swear: “We intended nothing but good!” Allah bears witness that they are surely liars.

9:108.t: Do not ever ˹Muhammad˺ stand ˹for Prayer˺ in it! Certainly, a mosque which was founded on Mindfulness from the first day is more worthy that you stand in it. In it are men who love to purify themselves; Allah loves those who purify themselves.

9:109.t: Is the one who founds his building on Mindfulness of Allah, and His Pleasure, better or he who founds his building on the edge of a crumbling cliff and it collapses with him in Hellfire—Allah guides not the ˹persistently˺ unjust people.

9:110.t: The building they have erected will always be a nagging suspicion in their hearts until their hearts are rent apart—Allah is indeed All-Knowing, All-Wise.

9:111.t: Verily Allah has bought from the Believers their lives and their money that theirs is Paradise; they fight in the path of Allah, kill and get killed. A promise onus on Him in the Torah, the Evangel and the Qur’an; Who is more fulfilling of His promise than Allah! Rejoice then in the merchandise which you have traded in—that is ˹truly˺ the great triumph.

9:112.t: ˹These are˺ The ˹truly˺ repentant, the ˹sincere˺ worshippers, the ˹ever˺ grateful, the ˹observantly˺ fasting, the ˹repeatedly˺ bowing down and prostrating, the enjoiners of virtue and advisers against what is unacceptable, and the keepers of the boundaries of Allah; give glad tidings to the Believers.

9:113.t: It is not for the Prophet and those who Believe to ask for forgiveness for the Associators – even if they were related – after it became clear to them that they are the companions of Hell.

9:114.t: Ibrāhīm’s asking for forgiveness for his father was only for a promise that he made him. When it became clear to him that he was an enemy of Allah, he disowned him—verily Ibrāhīm is ever imploring ˹with fervent prayers˺, most longanimous.

9:115.t: Allah would not misguide a people after He had guided them, until after making plain to them what they need to be Mindful of—verily Allah is All-Knowing of everything.

9:116.t: Verily to Allah belongs the dominion of the Heavens and Earth, He gives life and causes death and you have no ally or helper beside Allah.

9:117.t: Allah has relented towards the Prophet, and the Immigrants and the Helpers who followed him in the hour of undersupply, after the hearts of a group of them were about to swerve but He relented on them; verily He is Most Compassionate, Most Merciful towards them.

9:118.t: And ˹He relented˺ towards the three who were left behind, ˹not˺ until the land tightened around them despite its vastness and their own selves straitened them, and they became certain that there was no refuge from Allah except in Him; He relented on them so that they may repent—verily none but Allah is the All-Forgiving the Most Merciful.

9:119.t: You who Believe, be Mindful of Allah and be among the Truthful.

9:120.t: It is not for the people of Madinah and the herds people around them to remain back behind Allah’s Messenger nor to draw themselves away from him. That because they will suffer no thirst, ˹hard˺ toil, severe hunger in the path of Allah; nor would they tread a ground that infuriates the Deniers or get a portion of an enemy without it recorded for them as a good deed—verily Allah wastes not the reward of good-doers.

9:121.t: Nor do they give out any sum, small or large, or cross a valley, without it recorded for them, so that Allah may reward them ˹as much as they would be˺ for the best of what they used to do.

9:122.t: The Believers are not to mobilize wholly. Would there mobilize from every band of them ˹only˺ a group so that ˹the remaining ones would be able˺ to seek knowledge in religion and warn their folks upon their return ˹to them˺, might they be cautious.

9:123.t: You who Believe, fight the Deniers who are nearer to you; let them find sternness in you and know that Allah is with the Mindful.

9:124.t: Whenever a sura is sent down, some among them would say: “Who of you has this one increased his Faith!” But those who truly Believe, it has increased their Faith and they rejoice.

9:125.t: As for those in whose hearts is disease, it has increased them filth to their filth; they die as Deniers.

9:126.t: Do not they see that they are afflicted with trial once or twice every year; they repent not nor are they admonished!

9:127.t: Whenever a sura is sent down, they look at each other ˹as if to say˺: “Can anyone see you?” Then they turn away; may Allah turn away their hearts for them being folks who discern not.

9:128.t: There has come to you a Messenger from your own, on whom your adversity is hard, ˹deeply˺ concerned for you and compassionate and merciful to the Believers.

9:129.t: But if they turn on their heels, say ˹Muhammad˺: “Allah is sufficient for me. There is no God but Him; in Him I put my trust. He is the Lord of the Grand Throne.”

10:1.t: Alif, Lām, Rā’; those are the Signs of the Wise Book.

10:2.t: ˹How˺ Is it a wonder to people that We have sent revelation to a man of their own: “You shall warn people and give glad tidings to those who have Believed that they have a truthful footing with their Lord”. Those who have Denied ˹even˺ said: “Indeed this one is a magician, manifestly!”

10:3.t: Verily your ˹True˺ Lord is Allah Who created the Heavens and Earth in six days then Established Himself on the Throne; He conducts ˹all˺ affairs. There is no intercessor ˹with Him˺ except with His permission. That is Allah, your Lord, so worship Him—do you not take heed!

10:4.t: To Him shall be your return, all of you. ˹This is˺ The promise of Allah, truthful, ˹for˺ He indeed initiates creation and then brings it back so that He rewards those who have Believed and did good deeds with justice. ˹But˺ Those who have Denied for them are a boiling drink and a painful Punishment for their ˹persistent˺ Denying.

10:5.t: He is the One Who made the sun a glow and the moon a light and ordained for it stations so that you ˹may˺ know the number of years and calculation ˹of time˺. Never has Allah created that except for the Truth; He details the Signs for those who attain knowledge.

10:6.t: Verily in the alternation of night and day, and whatever Allah has created in the Heavens and Earth are Signs for the Mindful ones.

10:7.t: Verily those who hope not in meeting Us, contented themselves with the worldly life and took comfort in it; the ˹very˺ ones who are ˹deliberately˺ heedless of Our Signs.

10:8.t: These, their resort is the Fire for what they used to earn.

10:9.t: Verily the ones who have Believed and done good deeds, their Lord will guide them by their Faith; rivers flow under them in the Gardens of bliss.

10:10.t: Their prayer in it is “Glorified be You, our Lord”, their greeting in it is “Peace” and the concluding of their prayer is “˹All˺ Gratitude be to Allah, the Lord of all beings!”

10:11.t: Would Allah hasten evil to people as much as they ˹seek to˺ hasten good, their ˹given˺ term would have been terminated. ˹But they are given respite so˺ We leave those who hope not in meeting Us to wander aimlessly in their blindness.

10:12.t: When harm touches a human, he would pray to Us ˹whether lying˺ on his side or sitting or standing, but whenever We remove his harm from him, he ˹no sooner˺ moves on as if he did not pray to Us for a harm that touched him; thus has ˹always˺ been prettified for those of excess what they used to do.

10:13.t: Certainly We have destroyed ˹many of ˺ the generations before you when they wronged; their Messengers had come to them with clear evidences yet they would never Believe! Thus We requite the criminal people.

10:14.t: Then We made you successors in the land after them, so that We may see how you fare.

10:15.t: When Our Signs, distinctly evident, are recited to them, those who hope not in meeting Us say: “Come up you ˹Muhammad˺ with a Qur’an different from this one, or ˹else˺ modify it!”Say: “It is not for me to modify it out of my own accord! I only follow what is revealed to me! Indeed I fear – should I disobey my Lord – the Punishment of a Great Day!”

10:16.t: Say: “Had Allah wished, I would not have recited it to you nor made you aware of it; I had stayed among you an age before it, do you not comprehend!”

10:17.t: Who does greater wrong than, he who fabricates lies against Allah or rejects His Signs—indeed the criminals are never successful!

10:18.t: They worship besides Allah that which neither harms nor benefits them and say: “These are our intercessors with Allah!” Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Are you informing Allah of that which He knows not neither in the Heavens nor in Earth? Glorified and exalted is He above all they Associate ˹with Him˺!”

10:19.t: People had been nothing but one nation, and then they differed. Had it not been for a Word, already passed from your Lord, what they differ over would have been ˹soon˺ settled”.

10:20.t: They ˹further˺ say: “Would only a Sign come down to him from His Lord!” Say then ˹Muhammad˺: “The Unseen is for Allah. Wait you then and I shall be among you waiting!”

10:21.t: Whenever We make humans taste mercy after a hardship that touched them, no sooner, they work their wiles against Our Signs! Say ˹Muhammad˺ “Allah is swifter in wiles”. Indeed Our messengers write down what you scheme.

10:22.t: He is the One Who carries you through the land and at sea, until when you are in the vessels and they run them in favourable wind, and they rejoice in it, ˹suddenly˺ a gusty gale comes upon them and come upon them waves from everywhere and they become certain that they have been surrounded, they invoke Allah being devout in religion to Him: “Should You save us from this, we shall surely be among the thankful!”

10:23.t: Whenever He saves them, they no sooner wrongfully transgress in the land. O people, your transgression is ˹only˺ against your own selves; ˹It is no more than˺ a ˹passing˺ enjoyment of this worldly life, then to Us shall be your return and We shall tell you ˹the Truth˺ of what you used to do.

10:24.t: Indeed the example of the worldly life is nothing more than water that We send down from the sky;soon it gets mixed in the plants of the ground, the kind that people and animals consume, until when the land assumes its ornament and beautifies itself and its owners become sure that they are able over it, ˹there˺ comes upon it Our Command at night or in the morning and We make it mowed as if it did not thrive ˹only˺ yesterday—thus We detail the Signs for people who ponder.

10:25.t: Allah calls for the Abode of Peace and guides whoever He wishes to a Straight Path.

10:26.t: For those who do the best ˹of deeds˺ is the best ˹reward˺ and more;neither gloom nor ignominy will overcast their faces. Those are the Companions of Paradise—forever they abide therein.

10:27.t: But those who earn bad deeds, the requital of a bad deed is one of its kind; ignominy overcasts them and there will be none to protect them from Allah. As if patches of pitch-dark night cloud their faces—those are the Companions of the Fire; forever they abide therein.

10:28.t: On the Day We gather them all, then We say to those who Associated: “At your spot! You and your ˹idol˺ Associates!” Then ˹soon˺ We disassociated them ˹from each other˺; and their Associates said: “Never were you worshipping us!

10:29.t: Allah is sufficient as Witness between us and you that we were ˹totally˺ unaware of your worship!”

10:30.t: There every soul scrutinizes what it did before; and they are returned to Allah their Master, the Real, and what they used to fabricate deserted them!

10:31.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Who provides for you from the sky and from the land; or Who owns hearing and sight and Who brings out the living from the dead and brings out the dead from the living; Who conducts ˹all˺ affairs?” They shall say: “Allah!” Then say ˹Muhammad˺ “Would you not ˹then˺ become Mindful!”

10:32.t: That then is Allah your Real Lord; what is beyond what is real except misguidedness! Whereof then are you turned away!

10:33.t: Thus ˹Muhammad˺ the Word of your Lord has come due for those who contravened; that they would never Believe!

10:34.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Are there among your Associates one who initiates creation and then brings it back?” Say: “Allah initiates the creation and then brings it back; whereof are you then turned away ˹from the Truth˺?”

10:35.t: Say: “Are there among your Associates one who guides to the Truth?” Say: “Allah guides to the Truth!” Is the One Who guides to the Truth more worthy of being followed or the one who is not himself guided, unless he be guided. What is it with you; ˹look˺ how you judge!

10:36.t: Most of them only follow conjecture; indeed conjecture avails not against the Truth in the least—verily Allah is All-Knowing of what they do.

10:37.t: This Qur’an would never have been devised without Allah; but ˹it is˺ a confirmation of what came before it and a detailing of the Book. There is no doubt in it; ˹it is˺ from the Lord of all beings.

10:38.t: Or do they say: “He made it ˹all˺ up!” Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Then bring about a ˹single˺ sura of its like and call whoever you can apart from Allah ˹for your aid˺ if only you were truthful!

10:39.t: Nay, but they rejected what they did not come to ˹fully˺ know – its interpretation is yet come to them; like so those who came before them rejected—look ˹Muhammad˺ how was the ending of the wrongful ones.

10:40.t: Among them are those who ˹will in time˺ Believe in it and among them are those who do not Believe in it; your Lord Knows best the corrupters.

10:41.t: If they call you a liar, then say: “My doing is mine and your doing is yours; you are free from what I do and I am free from what you do”.

10:42.t: Among them are those who ‘listen’ to you; are you to make the deaf hear ˹you˺, even if they heed not!

10:43.t: Among them are those who ‘look’ at you; are you to guide the blind even if they ‘see’ not!

10:44.t: Verily Allah wrongs people naught, but people wrong themselves.

10:45.t: On the Day when He gathers them – as if they had not stayed ˹in life˺ except for one hour of the day – they recognize one another; those who rejected the meeting of Allah have lost—never were they ever ˹rightly˺ guided.

10:46.t: And whether We show you ˹Muhammad˺ some of what We promise them or terminate your life ˹before then˺, their return is to Us and then Allah is Witness over what they do.

10:47.t: For every nation there is a Messenger, whenever their Messenger comes, they will be judged amongst them with justice; they are ˹never˺ wronged.

10:48.t: They say: “When is this promise ˹coming˺, if you are truthful?”

10:49.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “I have no power ˹even˺ to harm or do myself well, except for what Allah wished. For every nation there is a ˹set˺ term; when their term is due, they cannot put it off an hour, nor hasten it

10:50.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “See you ˹not˺, if His Punishment comes upon you while you are sleeping at night or in broad daylight, what of it are the criminals in a rush for!”

10:51.t: “Would you ˹only˺ when it ˹actually˺ takes place Believe in it? Is it now? When earlier you ˹indifferently˺ sought to hasten it!”

10:52.t: Then ˹on the Day of Judgement˺ it is said to those who wronged: “Taste you the Punishment of eternity; are you not requited but for what you used to earn?”

10:53.t: And ˹more so˺ they seek out news from you ˹asking˺: “Is it ˹really˺ true?” say ˹Muhammad˺: “Yea! By my Lord it is definitely true; never will you outdo ˹Allah˺!”

10:54.t: Were any soul that wronged in possession of all that there is in Earth, it would have ransomed itself with it ˹on that Day˺. They concealed their ˹deep˺ regret when they saw the Punishment. They are judged amongst those with justice and they are wronged not.

10:55.t: Nay, but definitely ˹all˺ that is in the Heavens and the Earth belongs to Allah; nay, but definitely the promise of Allah is true, but most of them know ˹it˺ not.

10:56.t: He gives life and causes death and to Him you shall be returned.

10:57.t: O people! There has come to you an admonition from your Lord and a healing for what is in the chests; a guidance and mercy for the Believers.

10:58.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺:“In the Grace of Allah and in His Mercy, in that let them then rejoice; it is ˹immeasurably˺ better than what they hoard!”

10:59.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Would you ˹not˺ consider what Allah sent down to you as provision and you divided it into ˹some˺ lawful and ˹some˺ unlawful; say: did you Allah give you permission ˹for this˺ or do you fabricate falsities against Allah?”

10:60.t: What do those who fabricate lies against Allah think ˹of their fate˺ on the Day of Judgement! Indeed Allah is bountiful to people, but most of them are thankless.

10:61.t: Never will you ˹Muhammad˺ engage in an affair, nor should you recite ˹some˺ Qur’an thereof, or you ˹Believers˺ do a deed except Us being Witness over you, when you pour ˹your hearts˺ into it; neither is a mote’s weight – nor smaller or greater – stashed away from your Lord in Earth nor in the sky without it being in a clarifying book.

10:62.t: Nay, but the allies of Allah will have neither fear nor will they grieve;

10:63.t: those who have Believed and are used to being Mindful.

10:64.t: Glad tiding is theirs in the worldly life and in the Hereafter; there is no changing Allah’s Words—that is ˹truly˺ the great triumph.

10:65.t: Do not ˹Muhammad˺ be saddened by their speech; indeed to Allah belongs all honour—He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

10:66.t: Nay, but whoever is in the Heavens and whoever is on Earth belongs to Allah; what Associates do those who call ˹gods˺ besides Allah follow? Indeed they only follow nothing but ˹mere˺ fancies, indeed they are only guessing.

10:67.t: He is the One Who made the night for you to rest at and the day agaze. Indeed in that are Signs to those who listen.

10:68.t: And they said: “Allah got Himself a child!” Glory is His! He is the Free of Need; to Him Belongs whatever is in the Heavens and in the Earth. You have no authority to this ˹claim˺, ˹or˺ do you say about Allah that which you know not!

10:69.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Verily those who fabricate lies against Allah will never succeed”.

10:70.t: ˹For them is but˺ The enjoyment of the worldly life, then to Us is their return and then We make them taste the severe Punishment for their ˹persistent˺ Denying.

10:71.t: Recite to them ˹Muhammad˺ the ˹notable˺ news of Noah when he said to his people: “My people, if my staying ˹among you˺ and reminding ˹of you˺ of Allah’s Signs is ˹so˺ unbearable for you – then in Allah I ˹unshakably˺ trust – so deliberate your affair, ˹collectively˺ you and your Associates, and find no qualms about your affair, then administer it to me and afford me no respite!

10:72.t: But if you turn away ˹from my call˺, then ˹you know that˺ I asked you for no ˹financial˺ reward for it! My reward is only with Allah and I was commanded to be among those who submit”.

10:73.t: They declared him a liar and We delivered him and those with him in the ship – and We made them successors – and We drowned those who rejected Our Signs; look how was the end of the warned!

10:74.t: Then We sent after him Messengers to their people; they brought them clear evidences but they would never come to Believe in what they had earlier denied—like so We stamp on the hearts of the aggressors.

10:75.t: Then We sent after them Mūsā ˹Moses˺ and Hārūn ˹Aaron˺ with Our Signs to Pharaoh and his notables but they waxed arrogant; they were indeed criminal people.

10:76.t: When the Truth from Us came to them they said: “Verily this is indeed sheer sorcery!”

10:77.t: Mūsā said: “Do you say to the Truth when it came to you: “this is sorcery”, but sorcerers never achieve success!”

10:78.t: They said: “Did you come to us to divert us away from the ways of our forefathers and that eminence should be yours ˹both˺ in the land; We shall never believe in you!”

10:79.t: Pharaoh said ˹to his aides˺: “Bring me every greatly knowledgeable magician!”

10:80.t: When the magicians came, Mūsā said to them: “Cast whatever you will be casting!”

10:81.t: When they cast, Mūsā said: “What you bring ˹really˺ is magic, but Allah will render it worthless—verily Allah does not render useful the doing of the corrupters”.

10:82.t: Allah confirms the Truth with His Words, even to the acrimony of the criminals.

10:83.t: Then none but ˹a few˺ of the progeny of his people Believed in Mūsā; ˹yet˺ with angst from Pharaoh – and their ˹own˺ notables – lest he should put them to trial—verily Pharaoh is high and mighty in the land; verily he is among the squanderers.

10:84.t: Mūsā said: “My people, if you have Believed in Allah, then put your trust in Him if you have ˹truly˺ submitted”.

10:85.t: They said: “In Allah we trust! Our Lord, make us not a trial for the wrongful people.

10:86.t: And deliver us with Your Mercy from the Denying people”.

10:87.t: And ˹thus˺ We revealed to Mūsā and his brother that you ˹both˺ designate ˹certain˺ houses for your people in Egypt, and make your houses ˹face the˺ Qiblah, keep up the Prayer and give ˹you Mūsā˺ glad tidings to the Believers.

10:88.t: Mūsā said: “Our Lord, you have given Pharaoh and his notables ornaments and wealth in the worldly life for them ˹only˺ to divert ˹others˺ from Your Path. Our Lord, obliterate their riches and tighten ˹the seal˺ on their hearts so that they may not Believe until they ˹actually˺ see the painful Punishment”.

10:89.t: He ˹Allah˺ said: “Your prayer has been answered ˹Mūsā and Hārūn˺, so stay straight and do not follow the path of those who ˹truly˺ know not”.

10:90.t: And We whisked the Children of Israel across the sea, and Pharaoh and his soldiers pursued them out of ˹sheer˺ transgression and ˹unbridled˺ aggression. Until when drowning overtook him, he said: “I Believe that there is no God except the One Whom the Children of Israel Believed in; I am among those who submit!”

10:91.t: ˹Is it˺ Now! When you have rebelled before and you were among the corrupters!

10:92.t: Today We deliver you, ˹only˺ your body, to be a sign to those behind you; ˹but˺ many among people are oblivious to Our Signs!”

10:93.t: Verily, We have settled the Children of Israel in a truthfully laudable settlement, and We have blessed them with good provisions, ˹but˺ they differed ˹among themselves˺ not until ˹true˺ knowledge came to them—indeed your Lord shall adjudge among them on the Day of Judgement concerning what they used to differ over.

10:94.t: Should you ˹Muhammad˺ hence be in doubt about what We sent down to you, ask then those who have been reading the Book before you; verily the Truth has come to you from your Lord, so be not one of the hesitant.

10:95.t: Do not ˹ever˺ be ˹Muhammad˺ among those who have rejected the Signs of Allah lest that you should be among the losers.

10:96.t: Certainly ˹Muhammad˺, the ones over whom the Word of your Lord has come true, will never Believe;

10:97.t: not ever if every Sign was to come to them, until they ˹actually˺ see the painful Punishment.

10:98.t: Never a ˹single˺ town Believed ˹when its time had elapsed˺, and its Belief had been of benefit to it; except the people of Yūnus ˹Jonah˺, when they ˹sincerely˺ Believed, We lifted ˹the cover˺ of the Punishment of abasement off of them and We made them enjoy ˹life˺ for a certain period.

10:99.t: Had your Lord wished, the dwellers of Earth, all of them together, would Believe;do you ˹Muhammad˺ force people ˹in spite of themselves˺ till they become Believers!

10:100.t: It is not for a soul to Believe except by Allah’s permission; and He places blight over those who mind not.

10:101.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Look ˹at˺ what is in the skies and the land!” But Signs and warners avail naught people who never Believe!

10:102.t: Do they wait for nothing but the like of the days of those bygone before! Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Wait then! I shall be with you among those who wait!”

10:103.t: Then, We deliver Our Messengers and those who Believe; like so it is incumbent upon Us to deliver the Believers.

10:104.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “O people, if you are in doubt about my religion, then ˹I hereby declare that˺ I shall not worship those whom you worship besides Allah; but I do worship Allah Who terminates your lives and I was commanded to be among the Believers.

10:105.t: And ˹I was commanded˺: “Devote your face for the religion ˹being˺ rightly-oriented, and never be among the Associators;

10:106.t: do not invoke those besides Allah who can neither benefit you nor harm you. If you do so, then you are ˹surely˺ among the wrongdoers”.

10:107.t: If Allah touches you with affliction, none will remove it except for Him, but if He wants good for you, then there is no stopping His favour. He affects with it whoever He wishes of His servants—He is verily the Most Pardoning, Most Merciful.

10:108.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “O people, there has come to you the Truth from your Lord, so whoever is guided, is guided for ˹the sake of ˺ his own soul, and who is misguided, is misguided against it; I am not a guardian over you!”

10:109.t: Follow ˹you Muhammad˺ what is revealed to you and be patient until Allah passes His judgement—He is the best of those who pass judgement.

11:1.t: Alif, Lām, Rā’; ˹this truly is˺ a Book whose Signs are perfected and then they were spelled out; ˹it comes˺ from ˹One Who is˺ Most Wise, All-Knowledgeable.

11:2.t: ˹Clearly stating˺ That: “You ˹people˺ should worship none but Allah – I am ˹Muhammad˺ being to you a warner and a harbinger of good news –

11:3.t: and that you should seek your Lord’s forgiveness and thereafter repent to Him; ˹may˺ He make you enjoy pleasurably until a stated term and grant every good doer ˹the reward of ˺ his good doing”. But if you turn away, then ˹say Muhammad˺: “I fear for you the Punishment of a great Day.

11:4.t: To Allah you shall ˹all˺ return—surely He is Able over everything”.

11:5.t: Nay, but they bend their chests to hide from it. Nay, but when they cloak themselves in their garments, He Knows what they conceal and what they reveal—He is Knowledgeable of what the chests hide.

11:6.t: There is not a single moving creature on Earth except its provision being from Allah and He knows its place of stay and place of storage; all ˹the details˺ are in a clarifying Book.

11:7.t: He is the One Who created the Heavens and Earth in six days – while His Throne had been over Water – to test you, whom of you does better. Certainly should you ˹Muhammad˺ say: “You shall be resurrected after death”, those who Denied shall say: “This is nothing but evident sorcery!”

11:8.t: But should We delay the Punishment from them for a given duration, they shall say: “What holds it back!” Nay, but on the day when it comes upon them, it shall not be warded off from them; they got hit by what they used to ridicule.

11:9.t: Certainly shall We make a human taste mercy from Us and then wrench it away from him; certainly he is despondently despairing, habitually Denying.

11:10.t: But should We make him taste good fortune after an adversity that touched him, he shall say surely: “Away are the worst ones from me!” Verily he is ever exultant, ever boastful!

11:11.t: Barring those who are patient and do good deeds; these for whom are forgiveness and a great reward.

11:12.t: Perchance may you ˹Muhammad˺ forsake parts of what is being revealed to you and your chest feels constricted by it, should they say: “Would a treasure be sent down to him or an angel come with him!” You are none but a warner; Allah is Guardian over everything.

11:13.t: Or do they say: “He made it ˹the Qur’an˺ up!” Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Then bring about ten suras of its like, made up, and call ˹for aid˺ whoever you can, besides Allah, if only you are truthful”.

11:14.t: But if they do not respond to you ˹Believers˺, then know ˹for certain˺ that it was sent down with Allah’s Knowledge and that there is no god but Him; would you ˹not˺ submit!

11:15.t: Whoever cares ˹only˺ for the worldly life and its adornment, We will deliver ˹the reward of ˺ their deeds to them in full; they shall not be swindled naught ˹out of it˺.

11:16.t: These are the ones for whom is nothing but the Fire in the Hereafter; nullified are their deeds in it and futile is what they are used to labour ˹at˺.

11:17.t: Or would the one who has ˹clear˺ evidence from his Lord, which is attended by a witness from Him, and before it ˹was˺ the Book of Mūsā, a guide and mercy ˹be like the worldly inclined˺; those ˹well-guided ones truly˺ Believe in it ˹but˺ whoever of the confederates Denies it then the Fire is his appointment. So do not be ˹Muhammad˺ in doubt about it and ˹know that˺ it is the Truth from your Lord but most people Believe not.

11:18.t: Who is more unjust than he who fabricated lies against Allah. These will be displayed before their Lord and the ˹truthful˺ witnesses will say: “These are the ones who lied against their Lord; nay, but indeed Allah’s Damnation is ˹meted˺ on the unjust!”

11:19.t: Those who turn away from the Path of Allah and wish it be crooked, while they ˹staunchly˺ Deny the Hereafter.

11:20.t: These were never to frustrate ˹Us˺ in the land and they would never have allies besides Allah; Punishment will be doubled for them—they were never able to hear nor were they seeing.

11:21.t: Those are the ones who lost their souls and what they used to fabricate deserted them!

11:22.t: Truly in the Hereafter they are the most at loss.

11:23.t: Verily those who have Believed, done good deeds and ˹devoutly˺ humbled themselves to their Lord; those are the Companions of Paradise—forever they abide therein.

11:24.t: The example of the two parties is like the blind and deaf, and the seeing and hearing; are they equal as an example! Would you not ˹then˺ heed?

11:25.t: Verily We had sent Nūḥ ˹Noah˺ to his people ˹and he said˺: “I am indeed a clarifying warner to you,

11:26.t: that you should worship none besides Allah; indeed I fear for you a Day’s painful Punishment!”

11:27.t: Then the notables, who Denied, among his people said: “We ˹can˺ only see that you are a ˹mere˺ human like us; we ˹can˺ only see the ones who followed you are the riffraff among us, ˹clearly˺ acting at face value; we cannot see that you are any better than us, no, but indeed we believe that you are liars!”

11:28.t: He said: “My people! Would you ˹not˺ see that if I had evidence from my Lord and ˹that˺ He gave me Mercy from His Own, but you were blinded ˹away˺ from it, would we then charge you with it, even if you were loathing of it!

11:29.t: My people! I am not asking you ˹any˺ money for it. My reward is only with Allah and I shall not turn away those who have Believed, they shall ˹come to˺ meet their Lord, but I ˹can˺ see that you are ignorant people!

11:30.t: My people! Who shall come to rescue me from Allah if I were to turn them away; do you not heed!

11:31.t: And I would not say to you that I have Allah’s treasure troves with me; I have no knowledge of the Unseen. I would not say that I am an angel and I would not say about the ones who are despised in your eyes that Allah will grant them no good; Allah knows best what their hearts hold. ˹If I were to say this˺ Then I would be among the wrongdoers!”

11:32.t: They said: “Noah! You have argued with us and argued much! Bring forth what you promise us if ˹only˺ you were among the truthful!”

11:33.t: He said: “None will bring it forth to you except Allah, if He wills; you shall not frustrate ˹Him˺!”

11:34.t: My advising will benefit you naught, though I intended to be honest with you, if Allah wants to lead you to stray; He is your Lord and to Him you shall return!”

11:35.t: Or do they say: “He made it up”. Say ˹Muhammad˺: “If I made it up, then the crime I commit is ˹held˺ against none but me and I ˹solemnly˺ disavow the crimes you commit!”

11:36.t: And it was revealed to Nūḥ that none, any more, of your people will Believe beside those who have already Believed, so be saddened not by what they are used to doing.

11:37.t: Build the ship under Our Eyes and ˹according to˺ Our Inspiration, and do not address Me regarding those who committed injustice; they shall be drowned.

11:38.t: And ˹as˺ he was making the ship; whenever a group of the notables of his people passed by him, they ridiculed him. He said: “If you ridicule us, then we ridicule you as you ridicule ˹us˺”.

11:39.t: You shall come to know who will come by a punishment that shall disgrace him and shall be afflicted by an everlasting Punishment!”

11:40.t: Until when Our Command came and the clay oven spurted ˹water˺, We said ˹to Nūḥ˺: “Carry into it a pair of every kind, ˹members of ˺ your household – except the ones regarding whom the word was already passed – and the ones who have Believed”—but only few have Believed with him.

11:41.t: He said: “Get aboard; in the name of Allah is its sailing and its lying at anchor—verily my Lord is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.”

11:42.t: While it was sailing them in mountain-like waves, and Nūḥ called out to his son – while he was keeping away: “My ˹dear˺ son! Get aboard with us and do not be with the Deniers!”

11:43.t: He said: “I shall seek a mountain that should safeguard me from the water!” He ˹Nūḥ˺ said: “There is no safeguarding from Allah’s Command this day, except whom He has mercy on!” The waves came between them and he was amongst the drowned.
