11:44.t: And it was said: “Earth! Swallow your water! Sky! Cease off!” And the water sunk ˹into the ground˺, the affair was settled and it ˹the ship˺ settled on ˹Mount˺ Jūdī. And it was said: “Away with the wrongful people!”

11:45.t: Nūḥ called out to his Lord and said: “My Lord! My son is amongst ˹the members of ˺ my household and your Promise is the Truth; You are the Wisest of arbiters!”

11:46.t: He said: “Noah! He is not among your household; he is not a righteous deed, so ask Me not what you have no knowledge of; I admonish you lest you be amongst the ignorant!”

11:47.t: He said: “My Lord, I seek refuge by You that I should ask You that which I have no knowledge of. Should You not forgive me and have Mercy on me, I shall be among the losers!”

11:48.t: It was said: “Nūḥ! Disembark in peace by Us, and blessings on you and on nations ˹coming˺ from those who are with you; but ˹other˺ nations, We will make them enjoy ˹life˺, then a painful Punishment from Us touches them!”

11:49.t: These are among the notable news of the Unseen; We reveal them to you ˹Muhammad˺. Neither you nor your people had knowledge of it before this. So be steadfast; indeed the final round is for the Mindful.

11:50.t: And to ʿĀd ˹We sent˺ their brother Hūd. He said: “My people, worship Allah ˹alone˺, there is no god for you besides Him; you are none but lies weavers

11:51.t: My people! I am not asking for payment for it; indeed my reward is with none but the One Who originated me—do you mind not!

11:52.t: My people! Seek your Lord’s forgiveness and thereafter repent to Him; He shall send the sky pouring down on you profusely and add power to your power; turn not away as criminals!”

11:53.t: They said: “Hūd! You have come to us with no proof and we shall not abandon our gods based on your saying; we shall never Believe in you!

11:54.t: We ˹shall˺ only say that some of our gods overcame you with harm!” He said: “˹Then˺ I certainly call Allah to bear witness, and bear witness you ˹also˺ that I disavow that which you Associate

11:55.t: besides Him. So conspire against me, altogether, and afford me not respite!

11:56.t: Verily, I have put my trust in Allah, my Lord and your Lord. There is not a moving creature without Him taking it by the forelock; verily my Lord is on a Straight Path.

11:57.t: ˹But˺ If you turn away, then I have delivered to you what I was sent to you with. My Lord shall bring to succeed you other people and you shall not harm Him in the least—verily, My Lord is Trustee over everything!”

11:58.t: When Our Command came, We delivered Hūd and those who have Believed with him by Mercy from Us; We delivered them from a dense punishment.

11:59.t: That was ʿĀd; they renounced the Signs of their Lord, disobeyed His Messengers, and followed the command of every mulish tyrant.

11:60.t: ˹So˺ They were pursued by Damnation in this worldly life; and on the Day of Resurrection ˹as well˺. Nay, but ʿĀd did indeed Deny in their Lord; nay, but away with ʿĀd, the people of Hūd.

11:61.t: And to Thamūd ˹We sent˺ their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said: “My people, worship Allah ˹alone˺, there is no god for you besides Him. He initiated you from Earth and made you its cultivators so seek His forgiveness and thereafter repent to Him: verily, my Lord is All-Near, All-Answering.

11:62.t: They said: “Ṣāliḥ, we had hopes in you before this! Do you instruct us against worshipping that which our fathers ˹used to˺ worship! Indeed, we surely are in greatly unsettling doubt about what you call us to!”

11:63.t: He said: “My people! Would you ˹not˺ see that if I had evidence from my Lord and ˹that˺ He gave me Mercy from His Own, who would then come to my aid before Allah if I disobey Him; you increase me nothing but ˹perdicious˺ losing.

11:64.t: My people! This is the she-camel of Allah, a Sign for you, so let it graze ˹freely˺ in the land of Allah and touch it not with ˹any˺ harm; else a close by punishment will betake you”.

11:65.t: They slayed her, and he said ˹to them˺: “Enjoy ˹staying˺ in your land for three days; that is a promise, not lied!”

11:66.t: When Our Command came, We delivered Ṣāliḥ and those who Believed with him by Mercy from Us from the disgrace of that day—verily ˹Muhammad˺ your Lord is the All-Powerful, All-Prevailing!

11:67.t: The ˹mighty˺ shout betook the wrongful ones and they became ˹dead˺ fallen prone in their ˹very˺ land;

11:68.t: as if they had not ˹ever˺ thrived in it. Nay, but Thamūd did indeed Deny in their Lord; nay, but away with Thamūd!

11:69.t: Verily, Our messengers came to Ibrāhīm with the glad tiding. They said: “Peace!” and he said: “Peace!” Little did he linger to bring a roasted calf.

11:70.t: When he saw ˹that˺ their hands did not reach out for it, he deemed them strange and became apprehensive of them. They said: “Fear not. We have been sent to Lot’s people”.

11:71.t: His woman was standing; she laughed and We gave her the glad tiding of ˹the coming of ˺ Isḥāq and after Isḥāq ˹the coming of ˺ Yaʿqūb.

11:72.t: She said: “How bizarre! Shall I give birth when I am an old woman and this, my husband, is an old man! This, indeed, is a really strange thing!”

11:73.t: They said: “Do you find Allah’s Command bizarre! Allah’s Mercy and Blessings on you, people of the house—He is verily All-Praiseworthy, Most Glorious.

11:74.t: When alarm left Ibrāhīm and the glad tiding came to him, he argued with Us about Lūṭ’s people.

11:75.t: Verily Ibrāhīm is most long-suffering ever imploring ˹with fervent prayers˺ and devotedly repentant.

11:76.t: ˹The angels said:˺ “O Ibrāhīm, leave this ˹behind˺. Indeed your Lord’s Command has already come! Indeed a punishment against which there is no fending shall come upon them!”

11:77.t: When Our messengers came to Lūṭ, he became distraught and found them insufferable, and said: “This ˹truly˺ is a tough day!”

11:78.t: His people came to him rushing in a hustle – they were used to committing many a grave evil; he said: “My people, these are my daughters, they are purer for you! So be Mindful of Allah and do not bring shame upon me as to my guests! Is there not among you a level-headed man!”

11:79.t: They said: “You very well know that we have no right to your daughters. You exactly know what we want!”

11:80.t: He said: “If only I had power against you or could seek the support of unflinching backing!”

11:81.t: They said: “Lūṭ, we are the messengers of your Lord; they shall not reach you. So travel with your household in the small hours and let not any of you look back except your woman; she shall be afflicted with what they are ˹to be˺ afflicted with. Their appointment is the morn. Is not the morn near!”

11:82.t: When Our Command came, We turned it upside-down and We hailed them with stones of hard-set clay ˹raining˺ in close succession;

11:83.t: ˹labelled and˺ sealed with you Lord!” Nor are they ˹the stones˺ far from the wrongful!

11:84.t: And to Midian ˹We sent˺ their brother Shuʿayb. He said: “My people, worship Allah ˹alone˺, there is no god for you besides Him. Do not give short measure and weight ˹for others˺; I see that you are well off and I fear for you an enveloping day’s punishment”.

11:85.t: “My people, give full measure and weight fairly, do not swindle people out of the things that are ˹rightly˺ theirs and do not set about the land with ˹most˺ egregious corruption;

11:86.t: what Allah leaves out for you is better for you, if only you ˹truly˺ Believe—I am not a keeper over you.”

11:87.t: They said: “Shuʿayb! Does your Prayer bid you that we are to abandon what our fathers ˹used to˺ worship or to do with our money whatever we please! ˹What?˺ You! Who is the most sagacious, sensible ˹one˺!”

11:88.t: He said: “My people! Would you see that ˹I would deviate˺ if I had evidence from my Lord and ˹that˺ He provided for me handsomely! I do not seek to turn around your backs to commit that against which I bid you; I only seek to set matters aright, whatever I can. My success is only with Allah; in Him I trust and to Him I devoutly repent!”

11:89.t: “My people! Let not your opposition to me drive you into being afflicted with what befell the people of Nūḥ or the people of Hūd or the people of Ṣāliḥ; ˹yet˺ the people of Lūṭ are not far off from you!”

11:90.t: “Seek your Lord’s forgiveness and thereafter repent to Him—verily my Lord is Most Gracious, Most Benevolent.”

11:91.t: They said: “Shuʿayb! We do not grasp much of what you say! We can ˹only˺ see that you are powerless amongst us. Had it not been for your ˹immediate˺ clan, we would have stoned you! ˹Besides˺ You have no ˹great˺ regard in our sights!”

11:92.t: He said: “My people, do you have more regard for my ˹immediate˺ clan than Allah; and you staunchly put Him behind you! Verily, My Lord encompasses what you do!”

11:93.t: My people labour in the state in which you are as I will be labouring ˹my own way˺, for ˹sure˺ you will come to know to whom will come disgracing punishment and who is the liar! Await and I shall lie in wait with you!”

11:94.t: When Our Command came, We delivered Shuʿayb and those who Believed with him by Mercy from Us and the ˹mighty˺ shout betook the wrongful ones and they became ˹dead˺ fallen prone in their ˹very˺ land;

11:95.t: as if they had not ˹ever˺ thrived in it. Nay, but away with Midian as much as the Thamūd were cast away!

11:96.t: Verily, We have sent Mūsā with Our Signs and with compelling authority

11:97.t: to Pharaoh and his notables but they followed the command of Pharaoh; indeed Pharaoh’s command was never well-guided.

11:98.t: He heads his people on the Day of Judgement and have them come upon the Fire; awful indeed is the coming upon.

11:99.t: They were pursued in this one ˹life˺ by Damnation and on the Day of Judgement; awful indeed is ˹the˺ mounting ˹of Damnations˺!

11:100.t: That ˹is˺ of the notable news of towns, We relate to you ˹Muhammad˺; among them there are ones still standing and other ones mowed down.

11:101.t: We did not do them injustice, but they did themselves injustice. Their gods, whom they prayed to besides Allah, availed them nothing when your Lord’s Command came; indeed they increased them nothing but carnage.

11:102.t: Such is the seizing of your Lord when He seizes the towns while they are doing wrong; surely His seizing is painful, severe!

11:103.t: Indeed in that is a Sign to whoever fears the Punishment of the Hereafter; that is a Day for which people will be gathered—that is a witnessed Day!

11:104.t: We only delay it for a counted term;

11:105.t: a Day on which no soul will utter ˹a word˺ except with His permission; among them are the wretched and the cheerful.

11:106.t: As for the wretched, they are in the Fire; in it they have ˹such ghastly˺ inhaling and exhaling!

11:107.t: They will abide therein forever – as long as the Heavens and Earth last – except whomever your Lord wishes—indeed ˹Muhammad˺ your Lord is surely Doer of what He wants!

11:108.t: As for those who are made cheerful, ˹they are˺ in Paradise; therein they abide forever – as long as the Heavens and Earth last – except whomever your Lord wishes—a giving which is never cut off!

11:109.t: So, do not be ˹Muhammad˺ in doubt about what these worship. They only worship as their fathers used to worship in aforetimes. We shall bestow upon them their share in full, undiminished.

11:110.t: We had indeed given Mūsā the Book and it was differed upon. Had it not been for a Word, already passed from your Lord, they would have been ˹soon˺ judged amongst—verily they are in eerie doubt about it!

11:111.t: All ˹of these˺ your Lord shall surely requite them ˹for˺ their deeds—verily, Allah is Knowledgeable of what they do!

11:112.t: So be upright ˹Muhammad˺ as you were commanded as well as those who repented with you and do not transgress—verily He is All-Seeing of what you do!

11:113.t: And do not be titled towards those who committed injustice lest that the Fire touches you; you ˹would˺ have no allies besides Allah and then you would not be helped.

11:114.t: And keep up the Prayer at both ends of the day and at closely succeeding hours of the night; indeed good deeds dissipate the bad ones—that is a reminder for those who remember!

11:115.t: And be ˹steadfastly˺ unwavering as Allah wastes not the reward of good doers.

11:116.t: Never had there been among the generations before you those of ˹enduring˺ virtue who admonish against corruption in the land, except but few of them whom We had delivered among them! And those who did wrong ˹only˺ pursued what they were made to live lavishly in; they surely were criminals!

11:117.t: ˹Muhammad˺ Your Lord would not have unjustly destroyed towns when its people were into reforming ˹their deeds˺.

11:118.t: Had your Lord wished, He would have made people into one nation but they will ever be different;

11:119.t: except for those whom your Lord has mercy on. For that He had created them—your Lord’s Word has been consummated: “I shall certainly fill Hell from both jinn and humans ˹none exempted˺!”

11:120.t: And from all the notable news of the Messengers, We relate to you ˹Muhammad˺ what We brace your heart with. Indeed in this ˹sura˺ the Truth has come to you; and admonishment and a reminder to the Believers.

11:121.t: Say to those who do not Believe: “Labour in the state in which you are for surely we will be labouring ˹our own way˺!”

11:123.t: To Allah belongs the Unseen of the Heavens and Earth; all affairs shall be returned to Him. So ˹Muhammad˺ worship Him and put your trust in Him—verily your Lord is not unaware of what you ˹people˺ do!

12:1.t: Alif, Lām, Rā’; those are the Signs of the Elucidating Book.

12:2.t: We have indeed sent it down, ˹as˺ an Arabic Qur’an so that you may pay heed.

12:3.t: ˹Only˺ We recount to you ˹Muhammad˺ the best of stories by Our having revealed to you this Qur’an; indeed before it you were among those who are uninformed.

12:4.t: When Yūsuf said to his father: “O father! I have seen ˹in a dream˺ eleven planets and the sun and the moon; I ˹actually˺ have seen them prostrating to me!”

12:5.t: He ˹his father, Yaʿqūb, ˹Jacob˺˺ said: “My child! Do not recount your vision to your brothers lest they would plot some mischief against you; verily Satan is an open enemy to man”.

12:6.t: “Thus your Lord elects you, teaches you the interpretation of expressions and perfects His Favour on you and the house of Yaʿqūb like He perfected it on your fathers Ibrāhīm ˹Abraham˺ and Isḥāq ˹Isaac˺ before; indeed your Lord is All-Knowing, All-Wise.”

12:7.t: Verily, there had been signs in Yūsuf and his brothers for those who ask!

12:8.t: When they said: “Certainly, Yūsuf – and his brother – are more beloved to our father than us though we are a hardy band! Verily, our father is in clear error!”

12:9.t: “Kill Yūsuf or cast him away in a ˹faraway˺ land and your father’s face will be all yours and you shall be after him good doing folks!”

12:10.t: A voice among them said: “Do not kill Yūsuf but ditch him in the ˹hidden˺ cavity of the ˹unlined˺ well; some wayfarers will pick him up. That is if you are ˹really˺ bent on doing ˹it˺!”

12:11.t: They said: “Father! What is it with you; you do not trust us with Yūsuf! We are indeed honest to him!”

12:12.t: “Send him tomorrow with us to regale and play; verily, we shall indeed be his protectors!”

12:13.t: He said: “Indeed it saddens me that you would take him away, and I fear that some wolf would eat him while you are unaware of him!”

12:14.t: They said: “Should some wolf eat him with us being a hardy band, then we are truly losers!”

12:15.t: Then, when they took him away and they made up their mind to put him in the ˹hidden˺ cavity of the ˹unlined˺ well ˹they put him there˺, and We inspired him that you shall indeed tell them about this affair of theirs ˹only˺ when they are none the wiser.

12:16.t: And they came to their father ˹late˺ in the evening, weeping.

12:17.t: They said: “O father, we went racing and we left Yūsuf back with our belongings, and the wolf ate him. But you would never believe us although we speak the truth!”

12:18.t: And they brought ˹out˺ his tunic with false blood on it; ˹and˺ he said: “Nay, but yourselves have lured you into some matter. Then ˹it˺ shall be a comely patience ˹I hold on to˺ and Allah is the One Whose help is sought as to what you describe!”

12:19.t: Some wayfarers ˹later˺ came and they sent their water boy ˹to draw water˺; he lowered his bucket ˹then˺ said: “O what welcome news! This is a boy!” They hid him as merchandise; ˹but˺ Allah is All-Knowledgeable of what they do!

12:20.t: And they sold him off for a meagre sum, a few dirhams; they were indeed not keen on him.

12:21.t: The one from Egypt who bought him said to his wife: “Make his stay courtly; he may come to be of use to us or we may take him as a son”. Thus We made it becoming for Yūsuf in the land, and that We would teach him to interpret expressions; Allah is overruling in his affair but most people know not!

12:22.t: When he reached his prime, We bestowed upon him ˹sound˺ judgement and knowledge; thus We reward the well-doers.

12:23.t: And the one ˹woman˺ in whose house he was ˹tried to˺ entice him to herself; she tightly locked the doors and said: “Come onto me!” He said: “Allah forbid!” He is my lord; he made my stay comely! Never will the wrongdoers succeed!”

12:24.t: She indeed made for him and he for her had he not seen the clear proof of his Lord. Thus We ward off from him bad doing and vice; indeed he is one of Our chosen servants.

12:25.t: They raced to the door and she ripped off his tunic from the back and ˹by chance˺ they found her master at the door and she said: “What punishment is due for he who intends your spouse bad doing but he be jailed or a painful punishment ˹meted out on him˺!”

12:26.t: He said: “She ˹tried to˺ entice me to herself!” A witness of her ˹own˺ folks professed: “If his tunic is ripped off from the front then she tells the truth and he is among the liars;

12:27.t: but if his tunic is ripped off from the back then she lies and he is among the truthful!”

12:28.t: When he ˹her husband˺ saw his tunic ripped off from the back, he said: “It is verily ˹one˺ of your ˹women’s˺ guiles. Indeed your guile is great!”

12:29.t: “Yūsuf, put this behind you and you ˹woman˺ seek forgiveness for your sin; indeed you are among the wrongful!”

12:30.t: And some women in the city said: “The woman of the Chief Minister is enticing her boy to herself! He impassioned her with love! Indeed we deem her in clear error!”

12:31.t: When she heard of their cunning, she sent out for them and ˹carefully˺ prepared for them recliner couches and ˹deliberately˺ handed out each of them a knife, and said: “Come out before them!” When they saw him, they highly admired him, cut their hands and said: “Nay, by God! This is no human being; this is nothing but a noble angel!”

12:32.t: She said: “That you see is the one whom you censured me for! I indeed tried to entice him but he held himself aloof. Yet, if he would not do what I command him to, he shall verily be jailed and be among the despicable!”

12:33.t: He said: “My Lord, the jail is preferred to me over what they invite me for! And if You do not chase away their conniving from me, I may be swayed towards them and be among the ignorant!”

12:34.t: His Lord answered him and chased their conniving away from him—verily He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

12:35.t: Then it appeared to them, after they saw the signs, that they should jail him for a while.

12:36.t: And entered the jail with him two youths. One of them said: “Indeed I see me pressing wine ˹in a dream˺!” And the other said: “Indeed I see me ˹in a dream˺ carrying over my head bread out of which birds eat!” “Tell us of its interpretation; verily we see you among the well-doers!”

12:37.t: He said: “There shall not come to you ˹any˺ food, you are provided with, unless I shall tell you of its interpretation before it comes to you. That, you see, is ˹part˺ of what my Lord taught me; indeed I have left behind the religious way of folks who do not Believe in Allah; and verily they are Denying in the Hereafter!”

12:38.t: “And I followed the creed of my fathers: Ibrāhīm, Isḥāq and Yaʿqūb; we were not to Associate with Allah a thing of any sort. That is of the Grace of Allah on us and on people, but most people thank not”.

12:39.t: “My ˹two˺ jail mates, are diverse lords better ˹worshipped˺ or Allah ˹Alone˺, the One, the Overpowering?”

12:40.t: “You worship besides Him none but mere names you have named, you and your fathers, for which Allah has sent down no authority; the decision is for none but Allah. He decreed that you shall worship none but Him—that is the upright religion but most people know not”.

12:41.t: “My two jail mates; one of you will serve wine to his lord and the other one will be crucified and birds will eat from his head! The matter that you ask about has been ˹thus˺ settled.”

12:42.t: And he said to the one he thought would be spared of the two: “Mention me in your lord’s presence”. But Satan made him forget to mention ˹Yūsuf to˺ his lord, so he ˹Yūsuf˺ stayed in jail for several years!”

12:43.t: And the King said: “I see ˹in a dream˺ seven fat cows eaten up by seven lean cows and seven green ˹grain˺ ears and other dry ones. Enlighten me, O notables, as to my vision; if you ˹really˺ can interpret visions!”

12:44.t: They said: “Jumbled dreams; we have no knowledge of the interpretation of ˹such˺ dreams!”

12:45.t: The one of the two who had been spared – and he ˹only˺ recalled after a ˹very˺ long period – said: “I shall tell you its interpretation so send me!”

12:46.t: “Yūsuf, O ever-truthful one, enlighten us regarding ˹a dream in which˺ seven fat cows are eaten up by seven lean cows and seven green ˹grain˺ ears and other dry ones; may I go back to the people so that they may know!”

12:47.t: He said: “You will plant for seven consecutive years ˹as usual˺. Whatever ˹grains˺ you harvest leave them in their ears; except the little that you eat!”

12:48.t: “Then come seven tough ones; they eat what you had set aside for them; except the little that you keep secure.”

12:49.t: “Then after that comes a year in which people will be showered and in which they press.”

12:50.t: And the King said: “Bring him to me!” When the messenger came to him, he ˹Yūsuf˺ said: “Go back to your lord and ask him what of the affair of the women who cut their hands; indeed my Lord is All-Knowledgeable of their conniving!”

12:51.t: He ˹the King˺ said ˹to the women˺: “What was your business when you ˹tried to˺ entice Yūsuf to yourselves?” They said: “Nay, by God! We know of no bad doing of his!” The woman of the Chief Minister said: “Now the truth has become crystal clear. I did indeed ˹try to˺ entice him to myself; he surely is among the truthful!”

12:52.t: “That so he may know that I did not betray him behind his back; that Allah Guides not the conniving of the treacherous!”

12:53.t: “I do not absolve myself ˹thus˺. Indeed the self is ever commanding of bad doing, except those my Lord has Mercy on—indeed my Lord is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.”

12:54.t: And the King said: “Bring him to me. I shall have him solely for myself!” When he spoke to him, he ˹the King˺ said: “You are to us today of a great status; worthy of trust!”

12:55.t: He ˹Yūsuf˺ said: “Put me in charge of the treasuries of the land; I am indeed a good keeper; full of knowledge!”

12:56.t: Thus We made it becoming for Yūsuf in the land; he dwells thereof wherever he wishes. We impart Our Mercy on whomever We want and We do not lay to waste the reward of the good-doers!

12:57.t: Verily, the reward of the Hereafter is better for those who have Believed and have been Mindful.

12:58.t: And ˹when˺ Yūsuf’s brothers came and they entered upon him, he recognized them while they identified him not.

12:59.t: When he furnished them with their supplies, he said: “Bring me a brother of yours from your father. Can you not see that I give full measure ˹of provisions˺ and I am the best of those who accommodate!”

12:60.t: “If you do not bring him, there will not be any ˹further˺ measure ˹of supplies˺ for you with me and ˹do˺ not ˹ever˺ approach me!”

12:61.t: They said: “We will argue about him with his father; we shall surely do!”

12:62.t: He said to his servants: “Place their commodities in their saddlebags so that they may know them when they return back to their people. Perhaps they may return”.

12:63.t: When they returned to their father, they ˹no sooner˺ said: “O father, provisions have been withheld from us! So send our brother with us, may we obtain our measure; verily, we shall indeed guard him!”

12:64.t: He said: “Shall I entrust you with him only as I entrusted you with his brother before! Allah is a better Guard; He is the Most Merciful of all the merciful!”

12:65.t: When they opened their saddlebags, they found their commodities returned to them! They said: “O father, what more could we want! Our commodities have been returned to us, we ˹can further˺ cater for our households, protect our brother and we get a camel’s load ˹of provision˺ more! That is an easy measure ˹of provision to get by˺!”

12:66.t: He said: “I shall not send him with you unless you give me a pledge by Allah that you shall bring him ˹back˺ to me unless you are surrounded!” When they gave him their pledge, he said: “Allah is Keeper over what we say!”

12:67.t: He ˹Yaʿqūb˺ said: “My sons, do not enter ˹the town together˺ from one gate, but enter ˹it˺ from separate gates! I can ward off from you naught coming from Allah. Verily the decision is for none but Allah; in Him I put my trust and in Him shall the trusting put their trust”.

12:68.t: When they entered whereupon their father bade them, that would not have availed them to naught against Allah, except it being a matter in Yaʿqūb’s ˹innermost˺ self that he fulfilled. He was indeed knowledgeable as We had made him know; but most people know not!

12:69.t: When they entered upon Yūsuf, he drew his brother close to him. ˹And˺ He said: “I am indeed your brother; do not feel ˹anymore˺ miserable because of what they used to do”.

12:70.t: When he furnished them with their supplies, he placed the drinking cup in his brother’s saddlebag. Then a caller shouted out: “O you caravan! You have indeed stolen!”

12:71.t: They said – as they advanced towards them: “What do you miss?”

12:72.t: They said: “We miss the King’s measure and for whoever brings it, is a camel’s load”. “And I am guarantor of it!”

12:73.t: They said: “By Allah, you know that we did not come ˹here˺ to sow corruption in the land, neither were we ˹ever˺ thieves!”

12:74.t: They said: “Then what is his penalty if you are lying?”

12:75.t: They said: “His penalty – whoever’s saddlebag it is found in – he himself is its penalty; thus we penalize the wrongdoers!”

12:76.t: He ˹Yūsuf˺ began with their luggage before his brother’s luggage; then he extracted it from his brother’s luggage. Thus We planned for Yūsuf; he would never have been able to take his brother as per the King’s religion, except by Allah willing it so. We raise in rank whoever We will; above each possessor of knowledge an All-Knowledgeable One.

12:77.t: They ˹the brothers˺ said: “If he steals, then a brother of his had stolen before!” Yūsuf stifled it within himself and did not express it to them; he said: “But you are indeed in a worse position; Allah Knows best what you describe!”

12:78.t: They said: “O Chief Minister, his is a father who is a very old man, so take one of us in his place; verily we see you among the well-doers!”

12:79.t: He said: “Allah forbid that we take anyone except the one with whom we found our property with; so we would be unjust!”

12:80.t: When they ˹fully˺ despaired, they took themselves aside in conference. Their senior said: “Do you not know that your father took from you a pledge by Allah and before that you had relinquished Yūsuf! I shall not leave the land until my father permits me so or Allah passes a judgement for me—He is the best of judges!”

12:81.t: “Go back to your father and say: “O father, your son has stolen. We bear witness only to what we knew ˹for certain˺; we were never keepers over the unseen!”

12:82.t: “Ask the town at which we were and the caravan that we came with; we are indeed truthful!””

12:83.t: He said: “Nay, but yourselves have lured you into some matter. Then ˹it˺ shall be a comely patience ˹I hold on to˺; may Allah well bring them all to me—He is verily the All-Knowing, All-Wise!”

12:84.t: He turned away from them and said: “Woe is me over Yūsuf!” His eyes turned white with grief; he had been stifling ˹it up˺!

12:85.t: They said: “By Allah, you will never cease to remember Yūsuf until you are withered away or you are one of the dead!”

12:86.t: He said: “But I only moan my anguish and sorrow to Allah and I know via ˹revelation from˺ Allah that which you do not know.”

12:87.t: “O my sons, go and feel out for Yūsuf and his brother. Do not give up on Allah’s relief; none gives up on Allah’s relief except the Denying folks!”

12:88.t: When they entered upon him, they said: “O Chief Minister, we, and our households, have been touched by adversity and we have come with ˹some˺ paltry commodity so give us full measure ˹of provisions˺ and show us benefaction; indeed Allah rewards the benefactors”.

12:89.t: He said: “Do you know what you did to Yūsuf and his brother when you were ˹still˺ ignorant?”

12:90.t: They said: “˹But˺ Indeed, it is you, Yūsuf!” He said: “˹Yes˺ I am Yūsuf and this ˹indeed˺ is my brother. Allah has done us kindness. Indeed whoever is Mindful and shows patience, then Allah wastes not the reward of the well-doers”.

12:91.t: They said: “By Allah, Allah has favoured you over us. Indeed, we were mistaken!”

12:92.t: He said: “There is no reprimanding you today. May Allah forgive you; He is the Most Merciful of all the merciful!”

12:93.t: “Take this tunic of mine, cast it on my father’s face and he will regain his sight. And bring to me all ˹members of˺ your households.”

12:94.t: When the caravan pulled ahead, their father said: “Certainly, I perceive Yūsuf’s smell; if only you would not think me senile!”

12:95.t: They said: “By Allah, you are still in your error of old!”

12:96.t: When the bearer of glad tidings came, he cast it on his face and he regained his sight. He said: “Did I not tell you that I know via ˹revelation from˺ Allah that which you do not know”.

12:97.t: They said: “O father, seek forgiveness for our sins on behalf of us; we were indeed wrongful!”

12:98.t: He said: “I will ask my Lord to forgive you; He is verily the Most Pardoning, Most Merciful!”

12:99.t: When they entered upon Yūsuf, he drew his parents close to him and said: “Enter Egypt safe, by Allah’s will”.

12:100.t: And he raised his parents upon the throne, and they fell prostate to him. He said: “My father, this is the interpretation of my earlier vision. My Lord has made it true! He did me well when he got me out of prison and brought you from the ˹land of˺ the nomads after Satan has stirred ˹bad blood˺ between me and my brothers. Indeed my Lord is Subtle in what He wills—verily, He is the All-Knowledgeable, Most Wise”.

12:101.t: “My Lord, You have given me some Kingship and taught me some ˹of the˺ interpretation of expressions. O Originator of the Heavens and Earth, you are my Ally in the worldly life and in the Hereafter; bring my term ˹on Earth˺ to an end being devoutly submitted and join me with the pious”.

12:102.t: These are some accounts of the unknown, We reveal them to you ˹Muhammad˺. You were not with them as they resolved their matter while they were plotting.

12:103.t: And most people, despite your best efforts, will not Believe!

12:104.t: You do not ask them for any reward for it; it is no more than a reminder to all beings!

12:105.t: Just like any ˹other˺ Sign in the Heavens and Earth, they pass by it while they ˹deliberately˺ turn away from it.

12:106.t: Most of them Believe not in Allah without them Associating!

12:107.t: Do they feel secure that a shrouding of Allah’s punishment will come upon them or that the Hour comes upon them all of a sudden while they do not realize ˹it˺!

12:108.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “This is my path. I call to Allah, being clear-sighted; I and whoever follows me. Glory be to Allah; I am not among the Associators!”

12:109.t: We have sent before you none but men – to whom We reveal – among the dwellers of towns. Have they not walked the land and beheld how was the end of those before them! But verily the abode of the Hereafter is better for those who are Mindful; do they heed not!

12:110.t: Until the Messengers have ˹fully˺ despaired and they have become certain that they were lied to, Our victory comes to them and We deliver whomever We will. Our Might is not to be warded off from the criminal folks.

12:111.t: Indeed there has been a lesson in their stories to those of sound minds. It ˹the Qur’an˺ has never been fabricated speech, but a confirmation of what came before it, a detailing of everything and a guide and mercy to Believing folks.

13:1.t: Alif, Lām, Mīm, Rā’; those are the Signs of the Book; ˹verily˺ what was sent down to you ˹Muhammad˺ from your Lord is the Truth but most people Believe not.

13:2.t: ‘Allah’ is He Who elevated the Heavens – without pillars you see them – then Established Himself on the Throne and subjected the sun and the moon – each runs until a specified term; He conducts ˹all˺ affairs. ˹Thus˺ He details the Signs—may you ˹people˺ be certain of meeting your Lord.

13:3.t: He is the One Who spread out the Earth and made in it firmly-set ˹stabilizers˺ and rivers; and of all fruits, He made in it ˹Earth˺ a pair, two, of each; He shrouds night over day. Indeed in that are Signs to people who ˹ever˺ contemplate!

13:4.t: And on Earth there are adjacent plots ˹of land˺, gardens of grapes, ˹different˺ plants, and date palms – joined at the truck and unjoined – irrigated with the same water but We make some better than others in fruit; indeed in that are Signs to people who heed!

13:5.t: Should you ˹Muhammad˺ be astonished, then astonishing indeed is their saying: “Should we become dust, are we to be created anew!” Those are the ones who Denied their Lord; those in whose necks are yokes; those are the Companions of the Fire—in it they abide forever!

13:6.t: They ˹further˺ ask you ˹Muhammad˺ to hasten the evil before the good, while the examples have come to pass before them; indeed your Lord extends forgiveness to people despite their wrongfulness; indeed your Lord is severe in punishment!

13:7.t: And the Deniers say: “If only a sign were sent down to him from his Lord!” You are none but a warner; for each people there is a guide.

13:8.t: ‘Allah’ Knows what each female is pregnant with and what the wombs recede and increase with, and everything with Him is in ˹precise˺ measure;

13:9.t: the Knower of the Unseen and the witnessed; the Grand, the Ever-Lofty.

13:10.t: The same of you is any who keeps speech secret and who announces it, and who hides ˹impreciptibly˺ at night and who comes forth openly in daytime.

13:11.t: For him are alternating ˹angels˺ from his front and his back, guarding him by Allah’s Command; Allah alters not what a people are in unless they alter what is in themselves. Should Allah will evil for a people then there is no warding it off—theirs is no ally besides Him.

13:12.t: He is the One Who shows you lightning in fear and ˹hopeful˺ anticipation, and builds up the heavy clouds.

13:13.t: Thunder glorifies Him in Gratitude and the angels out of fear of Him; He sends thunderbolts and strikes with them whoever He wishes, yet they ˹still˺ argue regarding Allah—He is of mighty smites.

13:14.t: To Him ˹Alone˺ is the Call of Truth but those whom they call besides Him do not ever answer them with a thing; except like he who stretches out his hand for water to reach his mouth and it shall never reach it—the calls of the Deniers are but lost.

13:15.t: To ‘Allah’ prostrates ˹all˺ who are in the Heavens and Earth, willingly and unwillingly, and their shades early and late in the day.

13:16.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Who is the Lord of the Heavens and Earth?” Say: “Allah!” Say: “Do you then take for yourselves, besides Him, allies who neither possess benefit for themselves nor are they able of ˹warding off˺ harm!” Say: “Are the blind and the seeing ˹persons˺ alike; or darkness and light?”Or do they set up Associates for Allah who had created the like of His creation and then the creation confused them ˹which is whose˺! Say: “‘Allah’ is the Creator of everything; He is the One, the Overpowering!”

13:17.t: He sends water down from the sky and valleys flow according to their scope, and the flood carries floating scum; and scum like so is of what they stoke up fire on seeking ornaments and utensils. Thus Allah illustrates ˹the example of˺ Truth and falsehood; as for the scum, it goes disposed of but as for what benefits people, it settles into the ground—thus Allah illustrates examples.

13:18.t: For those who responded to their Lord is the best ˹reward˺. But those who did not respond to Him, if they had in their possession all that the Earth holds and double the measure, they would surely ˹seek to˺ ransom themselves ˹from the Fire˺ with it; those for whom is the worst ˹of calling to˺ account, their resort is Hellfire—ghastly indeed is this bed!

13:19.t: Is the one who knows that what has been sent down to you ˹Muhammad˺ from your Lord is the Truth like he who is blind; only people of sound reason ponder ˹this˺.

13:20.t: Those who fulfill ˹their obligations to˺ Allah’s commitment nor do they renege on the pledge;

13:21.t: those who join what Allah ordained to be joined, fear their Lord and are apprehensive of the worst ˹of calling to˺ account;

13:22.t: those who endure patiently ˹only˺ seeking their Lord’s Face, keep up the Prayer, spend ˹charitably˺ out of what We provided them with secretly and openly, ward off the evil with the good; those for whom is the ˹desired˺ alternation of the Abode;

13:23.t: Gardens of Eternity. They enter them – and whoever is righteous among their parents, spouses and posterity – and the angels enter upon them from every gate;

13:24.t: ˹greeting˺ “Peace be upon you for enduring patiently; how excellent is the alternation of the Abode!

13:25.t: Whereas those who renege on ˹their obligation to˺ Allah’s commitment after it has been set as binding, sever what Allah ordained to be joined, and sow corruption in the land; those for whom is Damnation and for them is the worst of Abodes.

13:26.t: Allah expands fortune for whoever He wills and He tightens; ˹yet˺ they are pleased with the worldly life whereas the worldly life, compared to the Hereafter, is but ˹a little˺ enjoyment.

13:27.t: And the Deniers say: “If only a Sign were sent down to him from his Lord!” Say: “Verily Allah misguides whoever He wills and guides to Him whoever returns ˹repentant˺”.

13:28.t: Those who have Believed and their hearts find comfort in mentioning Allah; nay, but in mentioning Allah do hearts find comfort!

13:29.t: Those who have Believed and did good deeds, the Comely is for them and the best of returns.

13:30.t: Thus We sent you ˹Muhammad˺ in a nation before whom many nations have come to pass, so that you may recite to them what We revealed to you but they Deny the Most Gracious.Say: “He is my Lord, there is no god but Him; in Him I put my trust and to Him repentant return!”

13:31.t: Had there ˹ever˺ been a Recitation ˹Qur’ān˺ with which mountains are removed or the ground rent asunder or the dead talked to ˹it would have surely been this Qur’an!˺; nay, but all affairs pertain to Allah.Did not the Believers give up hope ˹yet˺ that had Allah wished, He would have guided all people!For what they made, the Deniers would all the time be hit with a clamorous clout, or would it strike nearby their home, until Allah’s appointment comes—verily Allah does not break His appointment.

13:32.t: Indeed, Messengers before you ˹Muhammad˺ were ridiculed, and I allowed the Deniers time then I took them; how was then My Punishment!

13:33.t: Is He Who is Overseeing every soul for what it earned ˹be like who is not!˺; yet they set up Associates with Allah! Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Give them ˹attributive˺ names! Or are you ˹Deniers˺ informing Him of that about which He does not Know in the land? Or ˹are you claiming this˺ out of apparent discourse?” Nay, but the stratagems of those who Denied were prettified for them so they were turned away from the Path; whoever Allah sends astray, there is no guide for him!

13:34.t: Theirs is a punishment in the worldly life; but verily the Punishment of the Hereafter is more stringent—there will be none to shield them from Allah.

13:35.t: The example of the Paradise that the Mindful were promised, under which rivers flow; its fruits and its shades are everlasting—that is the alternation ˹of the Abode˺ of those who are Mindful, but the alternation ˹of the Abode˺ of the Deniers is the Fire!

13:36.t: Those to whom We had given the Book delight at what was sent to you ˹Muhammad˺; and among the confederates those who deny some of it.Say ˹Muhammad˺: “I was commanded to only worship Allah, Associating none with Him; to Him I call and to Him is my return!”

13:37.t: Thus We have sent it ˹the Qur’an˺ down as an Arabic Criterion;should you follow their whims after the knowledge that has come to you, there will not be an ally or shield for you from Allah.

13:38.t: Indeed We have sent Messengers before you ˹Muhammad˺ and We made for them wives and posterity. No Messenger can bring a Sign except with Allah’s permission; for every term there is a ˹set˺ record.

13:39.t: Allah erases what He wills and stipulates and with Him is the Mother of the Book.

13:40.t: ˹No matter˺ Should We show you ˹Muhammad˺ some of what We promise them or bring your life to termination ˹afore˺, you are only tasked with delivering ˹the Message˺ and on Us is the reckoning!

13:41.t: Could they not see that We come to the land and dwindle it from its sides; Allah decrees and there is none to repeal His decree; He is ˹indeed˺ swift in reckoning.

13:42.t: Those before them indeed plotted, but all planning is Allah’s own;He knows what every soul earns and the Deniers shall know for whom will be ˹desired˺ alternation of the Abode.

13:43.t: The Deniers say: “You ˹Muhammad˺ have not been sent!” Say: “Sufficient is Allah as Witness between me and you, and he who has knowledge of the Book!”

14:1.t: Alif, Lām, Rā’; ˹this is˺ a Book ˹that˺ We sent down to you ˹Muhammad˺, so that you ˹may˺ deliver people from ˹compounded˺ darkness into light, with the permission of their Lord, to the Path of the All-Prevailing, the All-Praiseworthy;

14:2.t: Allah to Whom belongs whatever is in the Heavens and whatever is in Earth; woe betides the Deniers from a severe Punishment ˹that awaits˺!

14:3.t: Those who favour the worldly life over the Hereafter, and divert away from the Path of Allah and wish it be crooked; those are in error, far-off!

14:4.t: Never had We sent a Messenger except in the tongue of his people so that he may clarify ˹the Message˺ to them. Allah then misguides whom He wills and guides whom He wills—He is the All-Prevailing, All-Wise.

14:5.t: ˹Thus˺ We had indeed sent Mūsā ˹Moses˺ with Our Signs that ˹you shall˺: “Deliver your people from darkness into light and remind them of the Days of Allah!” Indeed in that are signs for whoever is vastly patient, gratefully thankful!

14:6.t: As Mūsā said to his people: “Remember Allah’s favour on you, when He salvaged you from Pharaoh’s people as they subjected you to the worst of suffering; slaughtering your sons and keeping your womenfolk alive—in that indeed is a great trial from your Lord!”

14:7.t: “˹Remember˺ When your Lord declared: “Should you be thankful, I shall grant you more; ˹but˺ should you deny, ˹then˺ verily My Punishment is severe!””

14:8.t: And Mūsā said: “Should you, along with all those on Earth, Deny, then ˹know that˺ Allah verily is Free of Need, All-Praiseworthy!”

14:9.t: Had not the ˹notable˺ news of those who came before you reached you ˹people˺: the people of Nūḥ, ˹the tribes of ˺ ʿĀd and Thamūd, and those who came after them – none knows them except Allah; their Messengers came to them with clear evidences but they returned their hands into their mouths and said: “We ˹absolutely˺ Deny what you have been sent with; we are verily in doubt about what you call us for, seriously unsettling!”

14:10.t: Their Messengers said: “Is there doubt about Allah the Originator of Heavens and Earth! He calls you to absolve you of your sins and give you respite for a stated term!” They said: “You are but humans like us; you want to bar us from what our fathers used to worship! Bring us then evident authority!”

14:11.t: Their Messengers said to them: “We are indeed but humans like yourselves but Allah bestows Favour on whoever He wishes among His servants. It is not for us to bring you evident authority except by Allah’s permission! In Allah let the Believers put their trust”.

14:12.t: ˹But˺ Why would we not put our trust in Allah, when He guided us to our ˹right˺ paths. We shall surely endure your harming us patiently. In Allah let those who trust put their trust!”

14:13.t: And those who Denied then said to their Messengers: “We shall expel you from our land or you shall revert to our way of life!” Their Lord then revealed to them: “We shall surely destroy the unjust;

14:14.t: and We shall surely make you dwell in the land after them—that for whoever fears My Standing and fears My cautioning!”

14:15.t: They sought out a breakthrough ˹from Allah˺, and ˹so˺ let down was every headstrong tyrant;

14:16.t: Hell awaits him and he is given water of pus!

14:17.t: He sups it and can hardly gulp it ˹down˺; death comes to him from everywhere and never is he to die, and yet ˹more˺ harsh Punishment awaits him!

14:18.t: The example of those who Denied their Lord: their deeds are like ashes which the wind fiercely blows away on a gusty day; they can capture naught of what they earned—that indeed is misguidedness, far-off!

14:19.t: Have you not seen that Allah had created the Heavens and Earth with the Truth; if He wills ˹it˺, He would do away with you ˹people˺ and bring about a new creation!

14:21.t: And they, all of them, have emerged for Allah and the downtrodden said to those who waxed arrogant: “We had indeed been your ˹dedicated˺ followers, will you shield any of Allah’s Punishment away from us?” They said: “Had Allah guided us, we would have guided you! It is the same for us to bewail or bear patiently, there is no diversion for us!”

14:22.t: And Satan, when the affair has been settled, said:“Indeed Allah promised you the promise of Truth and I promised you but I failed you. I had not any authority over you, except that I called you and you responded to me. Blame me not ˹then˺ but blame yourselves. I shall not answer your shouts ˹for help˺ and you shall not answer mine. I hereby Deny your Associating of me ˹with Allah˺ aforetime”; “the unjust shall have a painful Punishment!”

14:23.t: And those who have Believed and done good deeds were admitted into Gardens under which rivers flow, forever they abide therein with their Lord’s permission; their greeting therein is: “Peace!”

14:24.t: Have you not seen how Allah sets as example a Good Word as a good tree; its root is firm and its branch is ˹shot up˺ in the sky,

14:25.t: it yields its fruit all the time with its Lord’s permission; Allah gives examples to people, may they heed!

14:26.t: And the example of a foul word is that of a foul tree torn up by from the ground; having no stability.

14:27.t: Allah makes firm those who have Believed with firm speech in the worldly life and in the Hereafter and Allah misguides the unjust; Allah does what He wishes.

14:28.t: Have you ˹Muhammad˺ not seen those who traded off Allah’s favour for Denial and settled their folks into the Abode of ˹utter˺ Ruination;

14:29.t: Hell, they taste it’s scorching – ghastly is the residence.

14:30.t: They set up ˹false˺ equals for Allah to divert ˹others˺ away from His Path. Say: “Enjoy! For indeed your return is to the Fire!”

14:31.t: Tell My servants who have Believed to keep up the Prayers and spend ˹charitably˺ out of that which We provided for them – both privately and openly – before a Day comes, when there will not be trading, nor bonds.

14:32.t: ‘Allah’ Whocreated the Heavens and Earth; sent water down from the sky and grew with it ˹all sorts of ˺ produce as provisions for you;subjected to you vessels running in the sea with His command; and subjected rivers to you!

14:33.t: And He subjected to you the sun and the moon unceasing; and He subjected to you night and day!

14:34.t: And He granted you out of everything that which you asked Him for. Should you ˹endeavour to˺ enumerate Allah’s Favour, you shall never round it up—verily man is gravely unjust, ever ungrateful!

14:35.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ When Ibrāhīm said: “My Lord, make this town safe and steer me, and my children, away lest we worship idols”.

14:36.t: “My Lord, they have led astray many a human! Whoever follows me, then he is of my own, but whoever disobeys me then You certainly are All-Forgiving, Most Merciful!”

14:37.t: “Our Lord, I have settled down some of my posterity in a plantless valley by Your Inviolable House; Our Lord, so that they may keep up the Prayer, so make hearts of some people fall for them and provide for them from ˹all sorts of ˺ produce, may they be thankful!”

14:38.t: “Our Lord, You verily Know what we hide and what we make public”;nothing ever is hidden away from Allah neither on Earth nor in the Heavens!”

14:39.t: “˹All˺ Gratitude be to Allah Who granted me Ismāʿīl ˹Ishmael˺ and Isḥāq ˹Isaac˺ in ˹my˺ old age—verily my Lord is All-Hearing of prayers!”

14:40.t: “My Lord, make me adhere to keeping up the Prayer and ˹some˺ of my posterity too. Our Lord, accept ˹my˺ prayers!”

14:41.t: “Our Lord, forgive me and my parents, and the Believers on the rising to Account ˹on the Day of Judgement˺!”

14:42.t: So think not ˹Muhammad˺ that Allah is unaware of what the unjust commit; He only delays them for a Day on which sights stare upwards ˹wide open˺!

14:43.t: ˹They will be˺ dashing forward, heads ˹fixed˺ up, their blinks never coming back to them, and their hearts gaping!

14:44.t: Warn people ˹O Muhammad of ˺ a Day when Punishment comes to them, then the unjust say: “Our Lord, delay us for a short while, may we respond to Your Call and follow the Messengers!” “Had you not sworn before, that there would be no parting for you!”

14:45.t: “And you dwelt in the dwellings of those who did themselves injustice, ˹only˺ when it became clear to you how We dealt them; and We gave you ˹many˺ examples!”

14:46.t: They plotted their ˹macabre˺ plot, ˹but˺ their plot is with Allah;˹but˺ even if their plotting was to remove mountains ˹it would not have unravelled the religion˺!

14:47.t: So think not ˹Muhammad˺ that Allah would ˹ever˺ renege on His Promise to His Messengers—verily Allah is All-Prevailing, capable of vengeance;

14:48.t: on the Day when Earth will be changed to another Earth and the Heavens ˹as well˺ and ˹when˺ they have emerged for Allah, the One, the Overpowering.

14:49.t: And you shall see the unjust bound on that Day in shackles;

14:50.t: their vestures are of tar and the fire overlays their faces!

14:51.t: That for Allah rewards every soul for what it earned; Allah is swift in reckoning!

14:52.t: This is a declaration to people, that they may be warned by it and to ˹fully˺ know that He is but One God, and that those of sound reason may remember!

15:1.t: Alif, Lām, Rā; those are the Signs of the ˹perfect˺ Book and a clarifying Recitation.

15:2.t: It maybe that those who Denied wish that they were Muslims.

15:3.t: Leave them ˹Muhammad˺ to eat, enjoy and ˹vain˺ hope beguiles them; they shall come to know!

15:4.t: Never have We destroyed a town without it having a known record;

15:5.t: never will a nation precede its ˹stated˺ term nor are they to remain behind.

15:6.t: And they said: “O you to whom the Reminder has been ˹successively˺ sent down! You are surely mad!”

15:7.t: “Wouldn’t you bring to us the angels if you are among the truthful!”

15:8.t: We only bring down the angels with the Truth; ˹and then˺ never will they be waited for!

15:9.t: Verily, We are Who have ˹successively˺ sent down the Reminder and verily for it We are Preservers!

15:10.t: Indeed We have sent ˹Messengers˺ before you for companies of old;

15:11.t: never does a Messenger come to them without them ridiculing him.

15:12.t: Like so We give it ˹Denial˺ passage in the hearts of criminals;

15:13.t: never are they to Believe in it ˹the Reminder˺, though the canon of the ancients already came to pass!

15:14.t: ˹Even˺ Had We opened up for them a door of the sky and they remained ˹constantly˺ ascending through it;

15:15.t: they would surely say: “Our sights have been closely shut. Nay but we are bewitched people!”

15:16.t: Verily, We have set positions in the sky and we adorned it for the onlookers.

15:17.t: And We preserved it from every outcast devil;

15:18.t: except one who eavesdrops, then follows him a flaring comet.

15:19.t: And the Earth, We have spread, cast in it firmly-set ˹stabilizers˺ and grew in it everything in balance.

15:20.t: And We made for you in it livelihoods and those for whom you provide not!

15:21.t: There is not a thing the treasures of which are not with Us and We send it down only in a known measure.

15:22.t: We sent the winds as pollinators, and We sent down water from the sky and watered you with it; you would not ˹be able to˺ reserve it.

15:23.t: Verily, We are Who gives life and causes death and We are the Inheritors!

15:24.t: And We surely know those of you who preceded and We surely know those who will come later.

15:25.t: Verily, your Lord ˹Muhammad˺; He gathers them—He is All-Knowing, All-Wise!

15:26.t: Verily We have created man from ˹clinking˺ dried clay, altered in colour and smell;

15:27.t: and the concealing one We had created before from the fire of pores.

15:28.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ When your Lord said to the angels: “I shall create a human ˹of skin˺ from ˹clinking˺ dried clay, altered in colour and smell”.

15:29.t: “When I had fashioned him and breathed in him of My ˹created˺ spirit, then fall prostrate for him!”

15:30.t: The angels then ˹immediately˺ prostrated all of them, except none;

15:31.t: but Iblīs. He ˹vehemently˺ refused to be among the prostrated ones!

15:32.t: He ˹Allah˺ said: “Iblīs! What makes you not among the prostrated ones?”

15:33.t: He said: “I was not to prostate to a human ˹of skin˺ whom You have created from ˹clinking˺ dried clay, altered in colour and smell!”

15:34.t: He ˹Allah˺ said: “Then get out of it; you are outcast!”

15:35.t: “And upon you is Damnation until the Day of Reckoning!”

15:36.t: He said: “Then my Lord grant me ˹time˺ until the Day when they are resurrected!”

15:37.t: He ˹Allah˺ said: “Then you are among the ones granted ˹time˺;

15:38.t: until the Day of the known time!”

15:39.t: He said: “My Lord! Then for Your misguiding of me, I shall make certainly ˹alluringly˺ adorned for them ˹life˺ on Earth and shall misguide them all,

15:40.t: except Your servants among them; the ones made ˹truly˺ devout!”

15:41.t: He ˹Allah˺ said: “This is ˹then˺ a Straight Path ˹leading˺ onto Me;

15:42.t: indeed on My servants you shall have no authority, except who follows you from among the stray ones!”

15:43.t: “Verily, Hellfire is their gathering place—all of them;

15:44.t: for it there are seven doors. For each door is a portion designated!”

15:45.t: Verily, the Mindful are in Gardens and springs.

15:47.t: And We have rooted out whatever ill-will in their chests. ˹So that˺ They are brothers on divans facing each other!

15:48.t: No fatigue shall touch them in it, nor shall they be ousted from it!

15:49.t: Notify ˹Muhammad˺ My servants that I am the All-Forgiving, the Most Merciful;

15:50.t: and that My Punishment is indeed the Painful Punishment!”

15:51.t: And notify them ˹Muhammad˺ about the guests of Ibrāhīm;

15:52.t: when they entered upon him and said: “Peace!” He said: “We are ˹very˺ wary of you!”

15:53.t: They said: “Do not be wary. We give you glad tidings of a greatly knowledgeable boy!”

15:54.t: He said: “Do you give me glad tidings ˹of a son˺ just when old age has touched me; what are you giving me glad tidings about!”

15:55.t: They said: “We gave you glad tidings of the Truth, so do not be among the despondent!”

15:56.t: He said:“Who would grow despondent of his Lord’s Mercy except thelost ones!”

15:57.t: He said: “And then what is your grave news, you sent ones?”

15:58.t: They said: “We have been sent to ˹some˺ criminal folks!”

15:59.t: “Except Lūṭ’s family; we shall deliver them all;”

15:60.t: “except his woman, we have deemed her among those remaining in doom”.

15:61.t: When the sent ones came to Lūṭ’s family;

15:63.t: They said: “Nay, but we came to you ˹with punishment˺ over what they used to doubt!”

15:64.t: “And We came to you with the Truth; we are verily truthful!”

15:65.t: So travel with your household in the small hours, and follow ˹them up˺ in their heels, let not any of you look back and proceed ˹to the place˺ where you are commanded.

15:66.t: And We decreed to him that these folks will be utterly obliterated ˹to the last one of them˺ at daybreak!

15:67.t: And the people of the city came to him full of high hopes.

15:68.t: He said: “These are verily my guests, so cause me no scandal!”

15:69.t: “Be mindful of God and do not disgrace me!”

15:70.t: They said: “Did we not forbid you from ˹paying host to˺ any person!”

15:71.t: He said: “These are my daughters, if you are bent on it!”

15:72.t: –by your life ˹Muhammad˺, they are verily wandering aimlessly ˹lost˺ in their drunkenness!

15:73.t: The cry betook them at their sunrise!

15:74.t: We then turned it upside-down and We hailed them with stones of hard-set clay.

15:75.t: Verily in that are Signs forthose who read signs!

15:76.t: And verily it is on a well-trodden path!

15:77.t: Verily in that is ˹enough˺ Sign for the Believers.

15:78.t: Verily the people of the thicket were unjust!

15:79.t: We ˹at once˺ took vengeance on them. Indeed both of them are on a clearly discernible road!

15:80.t: Verily the people of al-Ḥijr called the Messengers liars.

15:81.t: We gave them Our Signs but they were ˹constantly˺ turning away from them.

15:82.t: They were used to carve homes out of mountains ˹feeling thus˺ secure!

15:83.t: But the cry betook them at their daybreak.

15:84.t: Never was ˹then˺ what they used to earn to avail them!

15:85.t: We have created the Heavens and Earth with nothing but the Truth; the Hour is indeed coming so overlook ˹Muhammad˺ a gracious overlooking.

15:86.t: Verily your Lord ˹Muhammad˺; He is the Creator of everything, the All-Knowing.

15:87.t: Verily We have given you seven of the oft-repeated ones and the Grand Qur’an.

15:88.t: ˹Thus Muhammad˺ Do not linger your eyes on what We have made parties of them enjoy, do not feel sad over them and lower your wing for the Believers.

15:90.t: ˹Against a Punishment˺ Like ˹the one˺ We sent down on the dividers;

15:91.t: those who separated the Qur’an into divisions.

15:92.t: By your Lord then ˹Muhammad˺, We shall ask them all

15:93.t: about what they used to do.

15:94.t: Declare forthrightly then what you are being commanded with and turn away from the Associators.

15:95.t: We have indeed sufficed you against the ridiculers;

15:96.t: who set up with Allah another god—they shall come to know!

15:97.t: We may very well Know that your chest constricts over what they say.

15:98.t: So glorify your Lord in gratitude and be among those whoprostrate ˹themselves˺;

15:99.t: and worship your Lord until what is certain comes to you.

16:1.t: Allah’s Command has come so do not seek to hasten it; Glorified and High Aloof is He above all they Associate ˹with Him˺!

16:2.t: He ˹successively˺ sends down angels with the Soul – by His Command – to whoever He wishes of His servants, that: “You ˹Messengers˺ should warn ˹people˺ that there is no God but Me, so be Mindful of Me!”

16:3.t: He created the Heavens and Earth with the Truth; High Aloof is He above all they Associate ˹with Him˺!

16:4.t: He created man from a ˹mere˺ drop and lo, he is an articulate, ˹ardent˺ opponent!

16:5.t: And livestock He created; for you ˹people˺ in them you have warmth and ˹numerous˺ benefits, and from them you eat.

16:6.t: For you in them is ˹pleasure-inducing˺ beauty when you return back ˹late in the day˺ and when you set off ˹early in the morning˺.

16:7.t: They too carry your weights to a town that you would not have reached except with great pain—verily your Lord is Ever-Kind, Ever-Merciful.

16:8.t: And horses, mules and donkeys ˹are created for you˺ to ride on and ˹as˺ an adornment ˹for you˺; He creates that which you know not of!

16:9.t: ˹However˺ The showing of the ˹straight˺ path is on Allah, but some ˹paths˺ are skewed. Had Allah wished, He would have guided all of you.

16:10.t: He is the One Who sends down water from the sky for you; in it for you is drink and from it ˹He grows˺ the vegetation to which you send out ˹your cattle grazing fodder˺.

16:11.t: He grows thereof for you plants, olives, palm trees, grapes and of all ˹sorts˺ of produce; indeed in that is a Sign to folks who contemplate!

16:12.t: He subjected to you night and day and the sun and the moon; and the stars are subjected by His Command—indeed in that are Signs to folks who heed!

16:13.t: And all that He scattered for you in the land of different colours; indeed in that is a Sign to folks who reason!

16:14.t: He is the One Who subjected the sea so that you may eat from it tender flesh and from it you bring out ornaments that you wear; and you see vessels cavitating through it; ˹He subjected it˺ so that you may seek out His favours and that you may become thankful!

16:15.t: He cast in Earth firmly-set ˹stabilizers˺, so that it may not quake with you, and rivers and paths so that you may find your way;

16:16.t: and marks and by the stars do they find their way!

16:17.t: Is the One Who creates ˹then be˺ like the one who does not create; do you not think!

16:18.t: Should you ˹endeavour to˺ enumerate Allah’s Bliss, you shall never round it up; verily Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

16:19.t: Allah knows what you hide and what you make public.

16:20.t: Those whom they invoke besides Allah create nothing, but they ˹themselves˺ are ˹being˺ created;

16:21.t: ˹they are˺ dead not alive and they know not when they will be resurrected!

16:22.t: Your God is One God; yet those who do not Believe in the Hereafter, their hearts are in denial and they are being ˹inherently˺ snobbish.

16:23.t: Without a doubt, Allah knows what they hide and what they make public; verily He loves not the snobbish!

16:24.t: When it is said to them: “What has your Lord sent down?” They would say: “Scribbles of the ancients!”

16:25.t: Let them ˹then˺ carry all their ˹burdensome˺ sins on the Day of Judegment, and some of the ˹burdensome˺ sins of those whom they mislead out of ignorance; sordid indeed is the burden they carry!

16:26.t: Those before them indeed plotted, then Allah came to their building from the bases and the roof fell on them from the top; the punishment came on them whereof they knew not.

16:27.t: Then on the Day of Judegment, He disgraces them and says: “Where are My Associates whom you used to pit yourselves ˹against the Prophets and Believers˺ for their sake?” The ones who were given knowledge ˹then˺ said: “Verily disgrace and evil are today on the Deniers;”

16:28.t: whom the angels take their lives, being unjust to themselves! They then conceded defeat ˹and said˺: “We did not do any evil!”” Nay, but Allah is fully aware of what you used to do!

16:29.t: Enter you then the gates of Hell; forever you abide therein—miserable indeed is the domicile of the arrogant!

16:30.t: And it is said to those who had been Mindful: “What has your Lord sent down?” They said: “What is ˹abundantly˺ good!” For those who do well in the worldly life is a comely ˹reward˺. Yet the Abode of the Hereafter is ˹even˺ better—admirable indeed is the Abode of the Mindful!

16:31.t: Gardens of Eternity they enter; under which rivers flow. For them therein whatever they wish for—thus Allah rewards the well-doers.

16:32.t: Those whom the angels take their lives, being ˹full of˺ good. They say: “Peace be upon you! Enter Paradise for what you used do!”

16:33.t: Do they ˹the Associators˺ expect ˹nothing˺ but that the angels come upon them, or that your Lord’s Command comes! Like so did those who came before them do—Allah did not wrong them but they used to wrong themselves.

16:34.t: The evils of what they did befell them and they got hit by what they used to ridicule.

16:35.t: Those who Associate say: “Had Allah wished, we would not have worshipped anything besides Him, neither us nor our fathers; we would not ˹even˺ have forbidden anything without Him!” Like so did those ˹who came˺ before them do! Are the Messengers, then, tasked with anything but clear delivery ˹of the Message!

16:36.t: Verily, We had sent a Messenger in every nation, ˹to deliver˺ that: “You should worship Allah and shun false idols!” Among them were those whom Allah guided and among them were those whose dutiful lot was misguidance! So walk the land and behold how was the end of the deniers.

16:37.t: Keen as you are ˹Muhammad˺ on their guidance, Allah guides not whomever He sends astray; they will have no helpers!

16:38.t: They even swore by Allah with their most solemn oaths ˹that˺: “Allah resurrects not those who die!” Nay! It is a promise ˹due˺ on Him to bring true, but most people know not.

16:39.t: So that He shall explain to them that over which they differ, and so that those who Denied come to realize that they had been liars!

16:40.t: Our Saying to anything We want is only: “Be!” and it is!

16:41.t: Those who migrated for ˹the sake of˺ Allah after they had been dealt unjustly, We shall settle them a comely ˹settlement˺ in the worldly life; ˹but˺ the reward of the Hereafter is verily greater, if only they knew!

16:42.t: Those who bore patiently and ˹devoutly˺ put their trust in their Lord.

16:43.t: We had sent before you ˹Muhammad˺ only ˹mere˺ men, to whom We reveal; ask then the people of the Reminder, if you ˹people˺ know not.

16:44.t: ˹We sent them˺ With clear evidences, the ˹Sacred˺ Writs, and We sent down the Reminder to you ˹Muhammad˺ so that you may make clear to people what was ˹successively˺ sent down to them and so that they may ponder.

16:45.t: Do those who plotted evil ˹plots˺ feel secure that Allah would not make the ground give way with them or that the Punishment comes upon them whereof they perceive not!

16:46.t: Or that it should betake them during their goings hither and thither; never will they outdo ˹Allah˺!

16:47.t: Or that it should betake when they are apprehensive ˹of it˺; then indeed your Lord is verily Compassionate, Merciful!

16:48.t: Did they not consider all the things that Allah created, their shadows veering to the right and ˹the directions of˺ left; ˹compliantly˺ prostrating to Allah while being subjugated.

16:49.t: And to Allah prostrates whatever is in the Heavens and whatever is on Earth of a moving creature and the angels ˹do so˺; they disdain not ˹from it˺.

16:50.t: They fear their Lord, ˹Who is˺ above them and they do what they are Commanded to ۩.

16:51.t: Allah said: “Do not take up ˹worshipping˺ two gods. He is only but One God; so only Me be apprehensive of!”

16:52.t: To Him belongs whatever is in the Heavens and Earth and to Him belongs the religion eternally; Would you ˹then˺ dread any other besides Allah!

16:53.t: Whatever bliss you are ˹revelling˺ in is from Allah; then when you are touched by adversity to Him you bawl ˹out for help˺!

16:54.t: Then when He removes adversity from you, lo a group of you Associate with their Lord!

16:55.t: Let them be ungrateful for what We bestowed on them; enjoy, for you shall come to know!

16:56.t: They dedicate to whatever they ˹truly˺ know not about a share of what We provided them with; by Allah, you shall be asked about what you used to fabricate!

16:57.t: And to Allah they ascribe the daughters – Glory be His! – but to themselves ˹they own˺ what they like ˹most˺!

16:58.t: Whenever one of them is given the news of a female ˹newborn˺, his face stays clouded over while stifling ˹it up˺!

16:59.t: He cowers away from the folk, for the enormity of what he was given news of; shall he hold on to it in degradation, or slip it in the dust—sordid indeed how they judge!

16:60.t: For those who do not Believe in the Hereafter is the example of sordidness, but to Allah is the highest example; He is the All-Prevailing, All-Wise.

16:61.t: If Allah were to hold people to account over their injustice, He would not leave a single moving creature on its ˹the Earth’s˺ face. But He delays them until a specified term; when their term is due, they will not be delayed an hour nor will they be hastened.

16:62.t: They ascribe to Allah that which they abhor and their tongues describe falsehood: that theirs is the comely ˹outcome˺—without a doubt theirs is the Fire and that they will languish ˹forever therein˺!

16:63.t: By Allah, We had indeed sent ˹Messengers˺ to nations before you ˹Muhammad˺ but Satan made their doings look attractive to them so he is their ally this Day—for them is a painful Punishment!

16:64.t: We have only sent down the Book to you ˹Muhammad˺ to explain to them that over which they differ, and a guidance and mercy to people who Believe.

16:65.t: Allah sends water from the sky to enliven the earth with it after its death; indeed in that is a Sign to those who listen!

16:66.t: Verily, for you in livestock is ponderous consideration: We make you drink off of what is in their bellies – ˹coming˺ from between digested fodder and blood – ˹perfectly˺ pure milk; palatable for those who drink ˹it˺.

16:67.t: And from the fruits of palm trees and grapes you take intoxicants and comely provision; indeed in that is a Sign to those who are heedful!

16:68.t: Your Lord ˹O Muhammad˺ inspired the bees that: “Take up homes in mountains, and in trees and in what they ˹people˺ raise!”

16:69.t: “Then eat off of all fruits, then take the routes of your Lord, abated!” There comes from their bellies a drink of different colours; in it is cure for people. Indeed in that is a Sign to those who ponder!

16:70.t: And Allah created you and then He terminates your lives. ˹But˺ Some of you are deferred until the worst age, so that he knows nothing after the ˹much˺ knowledge gained— verily Allah is All-Knowledgeable, All-Capable.

16:71.t: And Allah favoured some of you above others in provision; those whom are favoured would not partake their provision with those whom their right hands possess and thus they become equal in it—do they deny ˹then˺ Allah’s favour!

16:72.t: And Allah made for you spouses out of your own selves and made for you out of your spouses children and grandchildren, and blessed you with ˹some˺ good provision—do they believe ˹then˺ in falsehood and in Allah’s favour they are resolutely denying!

16:73.t: They worship besides Allah those who possess no provision for them ˹neither˺ from the skies nor from the ground, nothing; never are they ever able ˹over that˺!

16:74.t: So do not pose ˹any˺ as similar to Allah; verily Allah Knows and you do not know.

16:75.t: Allah poses as example: a bondage slave who is able over nothing, and he for whom We have provided handsomely so that he spends out of it ˹both˺ secretly and openly; are they equal? ˹All˺ Gratitude be to Allah, but most of them know not!

16:76.t: And Allah poses as example two men:one of them is ˹born˺ mute, able over nothing, and is a liability to his master; wherever he directs him, he brings nothing of worth.Is he equal to he who enjoins justice while being on a straight path!

16:77.t: To Allah belongs the Unseen of the Heavens and Earth—the matter of the Hour is akin to the blink of an eye or ˹even˺ faster; verily Allah is Able over everything!

16:78.t: And Allah brings you out of your mothers’ bellies knowing nothing and He made for you the hearing, sights and hearts so that you may become thankful.

16:79.t: Did they not consider the birds, made prone to flying in the air of the sky, none holds them ˹from falling˺ except Allah—verily in that are Signs for those who Believe.

16:80.t: And Allah made for you out of your houses ˹sources of˺ settlement and made for you from the hide of livestock houses that you conveniently handle during your roaming and on the day when you set up camp. And from their wool, fur and hair furnishings and an enjoyment until a while.

16:81.t: And Allah made for you shades out of what He created, made out of the mountains dens for you, and made out for you apparel protecting you from heat and apparel that protects you from your ˹own˺ might—thus that He would perfect His favour upon you and that you may ˹devoutly˺ submit.

16:82.t: But if they turn away, then you ˹Muhammad˺ is tasked with nothing but clear delivery ˹of the Message˺!

16:83.t: They recognize the favour of Allah then they deny it; most of them are the ˹true˺ Deniers.

16:84.t: ˹Mention˺ The Day when We raise a witness from each nation,then those who Denied will not be granted permission nor will they be allowed to appease ˹their Lord˺.

16:85.t: When those who committed injustice come to see the Punishment;it will not be lightened for them neither will they have respite.

16:86.t: When the Associators see their Associates, they say: “Our Lord, these are our Associates whom we used to invoke besides You!” They ˹their Associates˺ then throw the speech back at them: “You are but liars!”

16:87.t: And they concede defeat to Allah on that Day and what they used to fabricate deserts them!

16:88.t: Those whoDenied and turned ˹people˺ away from the path of Allah, We have increased them Punishment over the Punishment for the corruption they used to spread.

16:89.t: ˹Mention˺ The Day when We raise a witness from each nation against them of their own and We bring you ˹Muhammad˺ as witness against these! We have sent down the Book as explanation for everything, a guidance and mercy, and glad tidings to the Muslims.

16:90.t: Verily Allah enjoins justice, the perfection ˹of deeds˺, imparting on relatives ˹their dues˺, and He proscribes ˹committing˺ vice, what is condemnable and aggression; He admonishes you to it ˹all˺, so that you may heed.

16:91.t: And fulfill the pledge of Allah when you make pledges and do not violate ˹your˺ oaths after they have been set as obligatory and that you made Allah as Guarantor over you—verily Allah Knows what you do.

16:92.t: Do not be like she who unknits her weaving, after its strength, into shreds, ˹by˺ taking your oaths ˹as a means˺ to gain leeway amongst you, ˹until˺ may ˹another˺ group, greater than ˹the˺ one ˹earlier˺ group ˹becomes ready for alliance˺. Allah tests you with it and so that He may explain to you what you differ over on the Day of Judgement.

16:93.t: Had Allah wished, He could have made you into one nation but He misguides whoever He wills and guides whoever He wills; you shall be asked about what you used to do!

16:94.t: Do not take your oaths ˹as a means˺ to gain leeway amongst you, lest a foot slip after it has become firm, and then you shall taste ugliness for your turning away from the path of Allah—yours will be a great Punishment!

16:95.t: Do not trade off Allah’s pledge for a pittance—what is with Allah is best for you, if only you knew.

16:96.t: What is with you depletes, but what is with Allah is everlasting; We shall confer upon those who endure patiently their reward ˹as much as they would be˺ for the best of what they used to do.

16:97.t: Whoever does good, male or female, while being a Believer, We shall make them live a good life and We shall confer upon them their reward ˹as much as they would be˺ for the best of what they used to do.

16:98.t: When you ˹Muhammad, are about to˺ recite the Qur’an, seek refuge with Allah from the outcast Satan.

16:99.t: Verily, he has no authority over those who Believe and are reliant on their Lord.

16:100.t: His authority is over none but those who take him as ally and by him they are Associators.

16:101.t: When We replace a Sign with another Sign – Allah Knows best what He ˹successively˺ sends down – they ˹would˺ say: “You ˹Muhammad˺ are a fabricator!” Nay, but most of them know not!

16:102.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “It has been ˹successively˺ brought down by the Holy Spirit” from your Lord with the Truth to make firm those who Believe, and a guidance and glad tidings to the Muslims.

16:103.t: We may very well know that they say: “He is taught by none but a human!” ˹How so when˺ The tongue of he to whom they slant is non-Arabic, while this ˹Qur’an˺ is an especially lucid Arabic tongue.

16:104.t: Verily, those who do not Believe in Allah’s Signs, Allah would not guide them and theirs is a painful Punishment!

16:105.t: None fabricates lies but those who do not Believe in Allah’s Signs—those are truly the liars!

16:106.t: Whoever Denies Allah after Believing – except the one who is coerced while his heart is comforted with Belief – but he who opens up his heart with Denial, then on them is Wrath from Allah and theirs is a great Punishment.

16:107.t: That for they prefer the worldly life over the Hereafter, and that Allah guides not the Denying people.

16:108.t: Those are the ones on whose hearts, hearing and sights Allah stamped; those are the truly unmindful.

16:109.t: Without a doubt, they are the ˹truly˺ losing ones in the Hereafter!

16:110.t: Then verily ˹Muhammad˺ your Lord is to those who migrated after they had been subjected to ˹severe˺ trial then they strived and bore patiently, then your Lord after that is verily All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

16:111.t: On the Day when every soul comes arguing for itself and every soul is given the full measure of ˹its˺ deeds; they shall not be dealt ˹with˺ unjustly!

16:112.t: Allah poses as example a village that was secure and assured ˹with˺ its provision coming plentifully from everywhere, then it denied Allah’s favours so Allah made it taste the overcast of hunger and fear for what they were used to doing.

16:113.t: Indeed there had come to them a Messenger of their own but they declared him a liar so the Punishment betook them for they were unjust.

16:114.t: Eat of what Allah provided for you, that is lawful and good, and be thankful for Allah’s favour; if only you do ˹truly˺ worship Him.

16:115.t: Indeed He ˹Allah˺ made unlawful for you but carrion, blood, swine flesh, and what was intended ˹as sacrifice˺ for others besides Allah; ˹yet˺ whoever is forced ˹by necessity˺ – neither transgressing nor going to excess – then certainly Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

16:116.t: Do not say to what your tongues describe, the falsity: “This is lawful and this is unlawful!” As such you fabricate lies against Allah. Verily those who fabricate lies against Allah will never succeed.

16:117.t: ˹For them is but˺ A passing enjoyment, and theirs is a painful Punishment.

16:118.t: And for those of Jewish faith, We prohibited what We narrated to you ˹Muhammad˺ before; We did not do them injustice, but they were unjust to themselves.
