16:119.t: ˹But˺ Then your Lord, ˹O Muhammad˺, to those who commit evil out of ignorance, then they repent afterwards and make amends, then Your Lord after that is certainly All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

16:120.t: Ibrāhīm ˹Abraham˺ was truly a leading paragon, devoutly committed to Allah, rightly-oriented, and he was ˹surely˺ not from among the Associators.

16:121.t: ˹He was˺ Thankful for His favours; He picked him and guided him to a straight path.

16:122.t: And We bestowed upon him ˹all˺ what is good in the worldly life, and verily in the Hereafter he is among the Righteous.

16:123.t: Then We revealed to you ˹Muhammad˺ that you are to follow the creed of Ibrāhīm, ˹who was˺ rightly-oriented and ˹verily˺ not from among the Associators.

16:124.t: The Sabbath was made ˹an obligation˺ only upon those who differed over it; verily your Lord, ˹O Muhammad˺, will judge between them concerning what they used to differ over, on the Day of Judgement.

16:125.t: Call to the Path of your Lord, ˹O Muhammad˺, with wisdom and gentle admonition, and argue with them in the best manner; verily your Lord Knows best whoever has strayed away from His Path and He Knows best the guided.

16:126.t: When you ˹Believers come to˺ inflict punishment, then inflict a punishment equal in measure to the punishment that was inflicted on you; but should you endure patiently then that is better for the patient.

16:127.t: Bear patiently ˹O Muhammad˺, but your patience is only through Allah, do not feel sad over them and do not feel troubled by their plotting;

16:128.t: verily Allah is with those who are Mindful and those who do good.

17:1.t: Glorified be He Who set His servant on nocturnal travel, by night, from the Sanctified Mosque to the Farthest Mosque around which We have blessed, so that We may show him of Our Signs; indeed He is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

17:2.t: And We had given Mūsā ˹Moses˺ the Book and made it guidance for the Children of Israel that: “You should not take any Trustee besides Me!”

17:3.t: “You who are the offspring of those We carried ˹in the ark˺ with Nūḥ ˹Noah˺; verily he was a greatly thankful servant!”

17:4.t: We had declared in the Book to the Children of Israel that: “You will surely sow corruption twice in the land and you will surely become greatly domineering”.

17:5.t: “When the promise of the first one has come, We would set upon you servants of Ours of steely mettle and they would diffuse through the lands; verily that is a promise coming to pass!”

17:6.t: “Then We would make the turn ˹of fortune˺ for you over them; We would supply you with money and children, and make you of greater summoning!”

17:7.t: “If you do well, you do well to yourselves and if you commit mischief, you commit it against them ˹yourselves˺. When the promise of the latter one was due, they (Our servants) would defile your faces, enter the Masjid as they had entered it the first time, and they would utterly wreck whatever they mount upon!”

17:8.t: May your Lord have mercy on you! But shall you go back, We shall then go back—We have made Hell an enclosure for the Deniers!

17:9.t: Verily this Qur’an guides to what is more straight, and gives the glad tidings to the Believers, who do good deeds, that theirs is a great reward;

17:10.t: and that for those who do not Believe in the Hereafter, We have ˹particularly˺ prepared for them a painful Punishment.

17:11.t: Humans pray for evil as much as they pray for good—indeed humans are ever rash.

17:12.t: We have made the night and day ˹as˺ two Signs.Then We effaced the sign of the night and We made the sign of day agaze, so that you may seek favour from your Lord and know the number of years, and calculation ˹of time˺—indeed We have particularly detailed everything!

17:13.t: To every human We have tethered his fate to his neck; and on the Day of Judgement, We bring out to him a book which he finds spread wide!

17:14.t: “Read your book, yourself is enough as a reckoner over you this Day!”

17:15.t: Whoever is guided, is guided for himself and whoever strays, he only strays against it; no burdened soul shall carry the burden of another—We would Punish unless We send a Messenger.

17:16.t: When We want to destroy a town, We command its pampered ones, they transgress in it and then the saying becomes true to it and We completely devastate it!

17:17.t: How many a generation have We destroyed after Nūḥ ˹Noah˺!Sufficient is your Lord ˹O Muhammad˺ as All-Knowledgeable, All-Seeing of the sins of His servants.

17:18.t: Whoever wants the instant one, We hurry for him in it whatever We wish to whomever We want, then We give him Hell, he roasts in it, reproachful and routed.

17:19.t: But whoever wants the Hereafter, and labours for it, its ˹true˺ labour, while being a Believer, then the labour of these is verily appreciated.

17:20.t: Both We provide for, these and these from your Lord’s giving ˹O Muhammad˺; your Lord’s giving is not ever withheld!

17:21.t: Look how We favoured some of them over others; indeed the Hereafter is greater in stations and greater in favouring!

17:22.t: Do not take ˹O Muhammad˺ besides Allah another god; lest you ˹ever˺ remain blameworthy and let down!

17:23.t: Your Lord has decreed that you ˹people˺ should worship none but Him and be beneficent to your parents.Should one of them or both of them grow old in your care, do not say to them ˹even˺ “ugh!”, do not tell them off, but say what is ˹considerately˺ respectful to them.

17:24.t: Lower the wing of humbleness out of mercy to them and say: “My Lord! Show mercy to them for they nurtured me when I was young!”

17:25.t: Your Lord Knows best what your intentions are,if you are righteous, then He is All-Forgiving for the sincerely repenting!

17:26.t: Bestow on the relative his due right as well as the destitute and the wayfarer;but do not waste ˹your money˺ away!

17:27.t: Verily wasters are the brothers of devils; indeed the devil is ever ungrateful to his Lord!

17:28.t: Should you turn away from them – anticipating mercy from your Lord that you hope for – then say to them something lenient.

17:29.t: Do not keep your ˹giving˺ hand shackled to your neck, nor stretch it all out,lest you become blameworthy, restrained!

17:30.t: Verily your Lord expands fortune for whoever He wills and He tightens ˹it˺—indeed He is always All-Knowledgeable, All-Seeing of His servants.

17:31.t: Do not kill your children fearing destitution; We shall provide for them and you; indeed their killing is ever a grievous mistake!

17:32.t: Do not come near adultery; verily it is a vice and sordid is the path ˹to take˺!

17:33.t: Do not ˹neither˺ kill the soul that Allah forbade, except with a right to it; whoever is unjustly killed, We have made for his agent an authority so let him not go to excess in killing; he will surely be made to prevail!

17:34.t: Do not ˹neither˺ come near the money of the orphan – except in a comely manner – until his coming of age;fulfil the pledge; verily a pledge will be asked after!

17:35.t: Give weights their full measure when you weigh and measure with a straight scale;that is better and is best in outcome.

17:36.t: Do not ˹neither˺ pursue that of which you have no ˹sure˺ knowledge;indeed the hearing, the sight and the heart, all of this, ˹you˺ will be asked about!

17:37.t: Do not ˹neither˺ walk about the land grandly; you shall not cleave the ground nor shall you top the mountains in height!

17:38.t: All of that – the worst of which – is resented by your Lord.

17:39.t: That is, of what your Lord ˹O Muhammad˺ revealed to you of wisdom;do not take besides Allah another god lest you may be thrown in hell; blameworthy, routed!

17:40.t: Did your Lord ˹O Deniers, specifically˺ confer sons upon you, and took ˹for Himself˺ the angels as females ˹daughters˺? What you utter is verily a grievous saying!

17:41.t: We have indeed spelled out ˹all matters˺ in this Qur’an, so that they may remember; ˹but˺ it increases them in nothing but aversion.

17:42.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “If there are other gods with Him – as they say – they would have ˹carefully˺ sought a path to the Owner of the Throne!”

17:43.t: Glorified and far exalted be He from what they say!

17:44.t: The seven Heavens and the Earth, and all that is therein, glorify Him;there is not a thing but glorifies Him in Gratitude, but you ˹people˺ discern not their glorification—verily He is ever All-Forbearing, All-Forgiving.

17:45.t: When you ˹Muhammad˺ recite the Qur’an We set between you and those who do not Believe in the Hereafter, a concealed veil.

17:46.t: And We put sheaths over their hearts lest they would perceive it, and in their ears there is impairment; when you mention your Lord ˹being˺ Alone, in the Qur’an, they turn on their heels in aversion.

17:47.t: We Know best what they listen through, when they listen to you, as they consort with each other, so the evildoers say: “You are only following a bewitched man!”

17:48.t: Look ˹Muhammad˺ how they pose examples to you! They have strayed, so they cannot find a path!

17:49.t: And they said: “Should we, when we are mere bones and remains, be resurrected as new creation?”

17:51.t: “Or something created that you find great in your chests ˹you shall even then be resurrected˺!” Then they would say: “Who would return us!” Say ˹Muhammad˺: “The One Who originated you the first time!” They would then jerk their heads at you and say: “When will it be?” Say: “May it be close!”

17:52.t: On the Day when He calls you and you respond in gratitude to Him, and you reckon that you only stayed a little!

17:53.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺ to My servants to say what is best, verily Satan stirs ˹bad blood˺ among them—verily Satan is ever an open enemy to humans.

17:54.t: Your Lord ˹people˺ Knows you best; if He wishes, He shows you mercy and if He wishes, He punishes you. We have not sent you ˹Muhammad˺ as guarantor over them.

17:55.t: Your Lord ˹Muhammad˺ Knows best those in the Heavens and Earth; We have favoured some Prophets over some and We granted Dāwūd ˹David˺ a ˹Sacred˺ Writ.

17:56.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Call on those you claim besides Him; they do not possess the ability to remove harm from you, nor would they be able to divert it!”

17:57.t: Those, to whom they pray, ˹themselves˺ seek a means to their Lord – ˹vying˺ whom of them gets nearer ˹to Him˺ – hope of His mercy and fear His Punishment; verily His Punishment ever guarded against!

17:58.t: There is no town which We would not destroy before the Day of Judgement, or Punish it a severe Punishment; that is ever noted in the Book!

17:59.t: Nothing withholds Us from sending with the Signs, except that the ancients denied them: We gave the Thamūd the she-camel, a Sign clear ˹to the sight˺, yet they wronged through her—We only send the signs to instil fear.

17:60.t: ˹Mention, Muhammad˺ When We say to you that your Lord has encompassing people. We have made the vision that We made you see nothing but a trial for people and ˹We made˺ the accursed tree ˹mentioned˺ in the Qur’an ˹nothing but a trial for people˺; We intimidate them, but it increases them in nothing but excessive rebelliousness.

17:61.t: ˹Mention˺ We said to the angels: “Prostrate to Adam!” They all prostrated except Iblīs. He said: “Should I prostrate to whom You created ˹as˺ clay!”

17:62.t: He ˹also˺ said: “Do You see this whom you honoured over me, should you delay me until the Day of Judgement, I shall certainly chew up his offspring, barring few!”

17:63.t: He ˹Allah˺ said: “Go forth! Whoever of them follows you, then Hell is your requital, a full measure requital!”

17:64.t: “Rouse whomever you can of them with your voice, charge against them with your mounted horses and your marchers, be their partner in ˹their˺ money and children and promise them!” Satan promises them nothing but delusion!

17:65.t: “Indeed on My servants you shall have no authority!” Sufficient is your Lord ˹O Muhammad˺ as Trustee!

17:66.t: Your Lord ˹O people˺ smoothly steers the vessels for you in the sea, so that you may seek of His favour—verily He is ever Most Merciful for you!

17:67.t: When you are touched by adversity in the sea, all that you pray to evades you except Him. But once He delivers you to dry land, you turn away—verily humans are ever ungrateful!

17:68.t: Do you ˹people˺ feel secure that He would not make the edge of the land give way with you, or send upon you a pelter; then you would not find yourselves an ally!

17:69.t: Or do you feel secure that He would not return you to it ˹the sea˺ once more, and set upon you a crusher of a wind and sink you for your ingratitude; and then you shall find your selves no pursuer against Us!

17:70.t: We have truly dignified the children of Adam and carried them both on land and in the sea, We blessed them with good provisions and vastly favoured them over many of those We have created.

17:71.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ The Day when We call each group of people by their leader; whoever is given his book in his right hand, then these read their book and are not dealt unjustly ˹even˺ with a filament!

17:72.t: Whoever is blind in this ˹life˺, then he is in the Hereafter ˹even˺ blinder and more astray in path.

17:73.t: They would have well swayed you ˹Muhammad˺ from what We have revealed to you, so that you may fabricate another ascribing it to Us; and hence they would have taken you as a bosom friend.

17:74.t: Had We not made you firm, you would have leaned to them a little.

17:75.t: So We would have made you taste doubly ˹tormenting˺ the life and doubly ˹tormenting˺ the death; you then would never find a helper against Us!

17:76.t: They would have surely stirred you from the land to oust you from it and so then they would have stayed behind you only a little!

17:77.t: This is ˹Our˺ Canon for the Messengers whom We have sent before you ˹Muhammad˺; you shall find no alteration in Our canon!

17:78.t: Keep up ˹Muhammad˺ the Prayer at the tilting of the sun until the darkening of the night; and ˹keep up˺ the recitation of the dawn, verily the recitation of the dawn is witnessed!

17:79.t: And at night keep awake ˹in Prayer˺ with it ˹the Qur’an˺, ˹as˺ supererogatory ˹Prayer˺ for you ˹O Muhammad˺, ˹so that˺ your Lord may resurrect you to a laudable position.

17:80.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “My Lord, enter me a truthful entry and exit me a truthful exit, and provide for me, from Your Own, an upholding authority!”

17:81.t: And say ˹Muhammad˺: “The Truth has come and falsehood has perished”. Verily falsehood ever perishes!

17:82.t: We ˹successively˺ send down of the Qur’an that which is a cure and mercy to the Believers; yet it increases the unjust in nothing but loss!

17:83.t: When We bestow favours upon a human, he turns away and strikes a distance ˹recoiling˺ to his side, but when evil touches him, then he is ever despairing!

17:84.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Let everyone act according to what he is like; your Lord Knows best whose path is better guided!”

17:85.t: They ask you ˹Muhammad˺ about the soul, say: “The soul is a matter of my Lord!” The knowledge that you ˹people˺ have been given is only but a little!

17:86.t: Had We wished, We would have done away with what We revealed to you ˹Muhammad˺; then you shall find yourself no ally against Us!

17:87.t: Except due to mercy from your Lord; verily His favour upon you is ever great!

17:88.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Should humans and the jinn rally up together to come up with the like of this Qur’an, they shall not come up with the like of it, even if they were backing each other”.

17:89.t: We have indeed spelled out for people in this Qur’an of each example but most people refused but to be extremely Denying.

17:90.t: And they said: “We shall not Believe in you ˹Muhammad˺ unless you make the ground burst with a spring for Us!”

17:91.t: “Or you have a garden of palm trees and grapes, and you then burst rivers gushing out profusely through it!”

17:92.t: “Or, ˹unless˺ you bring down the sky falling down on us – as you claimed – in chunks; or you bring Allah and the angels ˹right˺ in front of us!”

17:93.t: “Or, that you should have a house of gold; or you climb up in the sky, and even then we would not believe in your climbing unless you bring a book for us to read!” Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Glorified may my Lord be! Am I anything but a human, a Messenger!”

17:94.t: Nothing prevented people – when the guidance came to them – to Believe except their saying: “Did Allah send a human as Messenger!”

17:95.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Were there angels who would walk peacefully on Earth, We would have then sent for them from the Heavens an angel Messenger”.

17:96.t: Say: “Allah is sufficient as Witness between me and you; verily He is All-Knowledgeable, All-Seeing of His servants!”

17:97.t: The one whom Allah guides is the one who is truly guided, but those whom He sends astray, you shall not find allies for them besides Him! We shall herd them ˹flat˺ on their faces on the Day of Judgement; blind, ˹utterly˺ mute and deaf, their resort is Hellfire; whenever it abates We increase their scorching.

17:98.t: That is their requital for their Denying in Our Signs, and having said: “Should we, when we are mere bones and remains, be resurrected as a new creation?”

17:99.t: Did they not come to see that Allah, Who created the Heavens and Earth, is Able to create the likes of them; He appointed a term for them, there is no doubt about it—yet the unjust refused but to remain extremely Denying!

17:100.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “If you were in possession of the troves of my Lord’s Mercy, so surely you would have withheld, fearing to spend; verily humans are ever stingy!”

17:101.t: We had verily given Mūsā ˹Moses˺ nine clear Signs; ask then ˹Muhammad˺ the Children of Israel when he ˹Moses˺ came to them, and Pharaoh said to him: “I surely believe you are bewitched, Mūsā!”

17:102.t: He ˹Mūsā˺ said: “You know well that these were sent down by none other than the Lord of the Heavens and Earth, ˹as˺ eye-opener ˹evidences˺; and I well believe that you are wrecked, Pharaoh!”

17:103.t: He ˹Pharaoh˺ meant to stir them ˹Moses and his people˺ from the land, but We drowned him with those who were with him, all!

17:104.t: We said to the Children of Israel after him: “Dwell in the land! ˹But˺ When the promise of the latter comes, We bring you wholly, all and sundry!”

17:105.t: With the Truth We have sent it ˹the Qur’an˺ down and with the Truth it came down. We have sent you ˹Muhammad˺ as nothing but a bearer of glad tidings and a warner.

17:106.t: And ˹it being˺ a Recitation ˹Qur’an˺ We have elaborated so that you ˹Muhammad˺ may recite it to people with deliberation, and We Sent it down time after time!

17:107.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Believe in it or do not Believe in it ˹but˺ those who were given knowledge before it, when it is recited to them, swoop to their chins, prostrating.

17:108.t: And they say: “Glory be to our Lord; indeed, the Promise of Our Lord will ever come to be realized!”

17:109.t: They swoop to the chins crying and it ˹the Qur’an˺ increases them in ˹devoted˺ peacefulness! ۩

17:110.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺:“Pray to Allah or pray to the Most Beneficent, Whoever you pray to, His are the Most Magnificent Names!”;and do not raise your voice in your Prayers ˹O Muhammad˺ and do not quieten your voice in them, but seek a middle way between these.

17:111.t: And say ˹Muhammad˺:“˹All˺ Gratitude be to Allah, Who did not take a child ˹for Himself˺, nor has He ever had a partner in ˹His˺ Kingship, or an ally against humiliation; and exalt Him immensely!

18:1.t: ˹All˺ Gratitude be to Allah Who sent down the Book to His servant ˹Muhammad˺ and allowed no crookedness therein;

18:2.t: ˹it is˺ straightforward to warn against a mighty mettle from His Own and to give the Believers, who do good deeds, the glad tidings that theirs is a comely reward;

18:4.t: and to warn those who are of the saying: “Allah has taken ˹to Himself˺ a child!”

18:5.t: They are not in possession of ˹the slightest˺ knowledge of that neither were their forefathers—grave indeed is the word that comes out of their mouths; they say nothing but ˹sheer˺ lying!

18:6.t: Would you then ˹Muhammad˺ waste your life away after them in sorrow, should they not Believe in this discourse!

18:7.t: Verily We made whatever is on Earth an adornment for it so that We may test them (people) as to who of them does better deeds;

18:8.t: We shall be making whatever is on it a wasteland.

18:9.t: Or did you think ˹Muhammad˺ that the companions of the cave and the inscription were, of ˹all˺ Our Signs, ˹especially˺ wondrous!

18:10.t: ˹Mention˺ When the youths sought refuge in the cave then said: “Our Lord! Bestow Mercy of Your Own on us and make success the outcome of this matter of ours”.

18:11.t: Then We sealed their ears in the cave ˹for many˺ years numbered.

18:12.t: Then We brought them to life, so that We may Know who of the two parties is closer to getting right the duration of their stay.

18:13.t: We shall recount to you ˹Muhammad˺ their notable news with the Truth. They were ˹few˺ youths who ˹truly˺ Believed in their Lord and We increased them in guidedness.

18:14.t: We further made their hearts resolute, as they rose up and said: “Our Lord is ˹none but˺ the Lord of the Heavens and Earth! We shall not supplicate to any ‘god’ besides Him; otherwise our utterance would be ˹preposterously˺ too far away ˹from the Truth˺!”

18:15.t: “These are our folks ˹who˺ took ‘gods’ besides Him. Would they not then bring forth a compelling authority in their regard. Who then is more wrong than he who fabricates lies against Allah!”

18:16.t: “Since you have pulled yourselves away from them and what they worship other than Allah, seek refuge then inthe cave; your Lord shall spread for you ˹some˺ of His mercy, and make facile your affair.”

18:17.t: And you see the sun when it rises, it angles away from their cave to the right and when it sets, it draws away from them to the left; ˹while˺ they being in a spacious expanse in it (the cave). That is among the Signs of Allah;whoever Allah guides, then he is the ˹truly˺ guided one, but whoever He misguides, you shall not find him a guiding ally!

18:18.t: And you think them awake while they are fast asleep; We turn them ˹once˺ to their right sides and ˹once˺ to their left sides; and their dog extending its forepaws while at the threshold ˹of the cave˺. Should you peep at them, you would have surely fled away from them beating a retreat and would have surely been filled with horror of them!

18:19.t: And like so We brought them to life so that they would question each other. One among them said: “How long for did you stay ˹asleep˺?” They said: “We ˹may have˺ stayed for a day or part of a day!” ˹Then˺ They said: “Your Lord knows best the duration of your stay! Send one of you with these silver coins of yours to the town. Let him find out the purest food and get us some provision from it. Let him be gentle and let not anyone sense him!”

18:20.t: “Should they get the better of you, they would surely stone you or turn you back to their creed—you shall not ever prosper thereafter!”

18:21.t: And like so We made them chanced upon so that they may come to know that the promise of Allah is true and that there is no doubt about ˹the coming of˺ the Hour; ˹that˺ as they were disputing their affair amongst themselves. They said: “Put up some building around them; their Lord Knows best about them!” ˹But˺ Those who prevailed over their affair said: “We shall have a place of prostration put up above them!”

18:22.t: They would say: “˹They are˺ Three with their dog as their fourth!” And they say: “˹They are˺ Five with their dog as their sixth!” – merely shooting in the dark – And they say: “˹They are˺ Seven with their dog as their eighth!” Say ˹Muhammad˺: “My Lord Knows best their number!” None know ˹for sure˺ their number but a few. Do not ˹Muhammad˺ argue ˹with anyone˺ in their regard, except cursorily. Do not ˹neither˺ seek the counsel from any of those about them.

18:23.t: Say not ˹Muhammad˺ about anything: “I shall do that tomorrow!”

18:24.t: Except that your Lord wills ˹it so˺.Remember your Lord when you forget and say: “May my Lord well direct me to what is nearer to guidedness than this!”

18:25.t: They remained in their cave for three hundred years and ˹another˺ nine more added to them.

18:26.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Allah Knows best the duration of their stay. To Him belongs the Unseen in the Heavens and Earth!” How Perfect His Sight and how Perfect His Hearing are! They have no ally besides Him; He shares not His Decree with anyone!

18:27.t: Recite ˹Muhammad˺ what has been revealed to you of the Book of your Lord; there is no changing His Words. You shall find no Shelter besides Him!

18:28.t: Constrain yourself in patience with those who pray to their Lord early and late in the day seeking ˹only˺ His Face; do not ˹neither˺ avert your eyes from them seeking ˹out˺ the adornment of the worldly life; and do not obey whoever We made his heart ignorant of Our remembrance thus followed his vain desires and ˹hence all˺ his affairs are in shambles.

18:29.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “˹This is˺ the Truth from your Lord. Whoever wishes, let him Believe and whoever wishes, let him Deny!”We have especially prepared a Fire for the unjust, whose wall closes in on them and if they shout out for help, they will be helped with water; a smoldering, putrid fluid that sears faces—miserable indeed is the drink and sordid indeed is the retreat!

18:30.t: Verily, ˹as for˺ those who have Believed and done good deeds, We indeed do not render useless the reward of he who does well.

18:31.t: Those for whom are Gardens of Eternity; rivers running underneath them. Therein they are adorned with golden bracelets and put on green robes of sundus (fine silk) and istabraq (brocade) ˹while˺ reclining therein on divans—awesome indeed is the prize and splendid indeed is the retreat!

18:32.t: Set ˹Muhammad˺ for them as example two men. For one of them We provided two vineyards, surrounded them with palm trees, provided ˹various˺ plants between them;

18:33.t: –both vineyards yielded its harvest, holding back none of it – and We sent gushing a river ˹running˺ through them!

18:34.t: He had ˹a great harvest of˺ fruits as he said to his fellow while arguing with him: “I am of a greater wealth and summoning than you!”

18:35.t: He entered his garden – being unjust to himself – and said: “I believe not that this shall ever decay!”

18:36.t: “And I think not that the Hour is ever coming. Shall I ever ˹perchance˺ be returned to my Lord, mine shall be a better rebound!”

18:37.t: His fellow said while arguing with him: “Did you Deny your Lord Who created you from dust, then from a drop, them perfectly formed you into a man!”

18:38.t: “But I ˹declare that˺ He is Allah, my Lord and I shall not Associate any with my Lord!”

18:39.t: “˹Why˺ Wouldn’t you, when you enter your garden, say: “˹This is˺ What Allah willed. There is no power except through Allah!” Or did you see me below you in wealth and children?”

18:40.t: “May my Lord well give me what is better than your garden and send on it arrows from the sky and it becomes a slippery wasteland!”

18:41.t: “Or its water seeps deep in the ground so that you cannot ever retrieve it!”

18:42.t: His fruits were surrounded from all sides and he got to turning his palms ˹upwards and downwards in despair˺ over what he spent in it (the garden) as it went to rack and ruin, saying: “O, how I wish I had not Associated any with my Lord!”

18:43.t: He had no clique to sustain him against Allah; he would not ever have been sustained!

18:44.t: There and then, the ˹true˺ helping out is Allah’s ˹Alone˺, the True; His is a better prize and His is a better outcome!

18:45.t: Set ˹Muhammad˺ for them the example of the worldly life as water which We send down from the sky, the plants of the land fuse with it, and soon it becomes brittle, ˹readily˺ scattered by the winds; Allah is ever Most-Able over everything.

18:46.t: Money and sons are the adornment of the worldly life, but the everlasting good deeds are a better prize with your Lord and are a better hope.

18:47.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ The Day when We float the mountains away and you see the land all evident, and We gather them leaving out none of them!

18:48.t: They are displayed to your Lord in a row; “Here you have come to Us as We have created you the first time. Nay, but you claimed that We would not make you an appointment!”

18:49.t: The book is laid down, and you see the criminals apprehensive of what is ˹written down˺ in it and say: “Woe be us! What is the matter with this book! It leaves out not a matter, small or big, without noting it down!” They found what they committed present—your Lord never does anyone an injustice!

18:50.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ When We said to the angels: “Prostrate to Adam!” They ˹all˺ prostrated except Iblīs; he was one of the jinn, and he rebelled against the Command of his Lord! Do you then take him and his offspring as allies instead of Me while they being an enemy to you!Sordid indeed is the exchange of the unjust!

18:51.t: I have not made them (the devils) witness the creation of the Heavens and Earth nor the creation of their own selves; I would not have taken as aides those who guide astray!

18:52.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺ The Day when He says: “Call out My Associates that you claimed!” They called out for them but they did not respond to them and We set up a ˹ruinous˺ barrier between them.

18:53.t: ˹There˺ The criminals saw the Fire and realized that they are ˹inevitably˺ falling into it; they found no breakout from it!

18:54.t: We have expounded in this Qur’an for humans all sorts of examples but humans are the most argumentative thing!

18:55.t: Nothing withheld people from Believing, when guidance came to them, and to seek their Lord’s forgiveness, barring that the canon of the ancients befalls them or that the Punishment comes upon them barefaced.

18:56.t: We only send Messengers as bearers of glad tidings and warners; yet those who are Denying argue with falsehood ˹seeking˺ to rout the Truth with it, and they took My Signs and what they have been warned against for mockery!

18:57.t: Who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the Signs of his Lord and he turns away from them forgetting what his hands had sent forth; verily We have put sheathes upon their hearts lest that they should comprehend it (the Qur’an) and in their ears there is an impairment. Should you ˹Muhammad˺ call them to guidance, so they would not ever find guidance.

18:58.t: Your Lord, the All-Forgiving, the Possessor of Mercy, should He bring them to account over what they have ˹truly˺ earned, He would have hasted the Punishment to them. Nay, but they have a ˹set˺ appointment, they would not find an escape from it!

18:59.t: Those ˹are the˺ towns ˹that˺ We have destroyed when they committed injustice, and We made an appointment for their destruction.

18:60.t: ˹Mention Muhammad˺When Mūsā (Moses) said to his ˹servant˺ boy: “I shall not cease until I reach the joining of the two seas or ˹if not˺ I shall keep moving ˹on˺ for times of length!”

18:61.t: When they reached the joining of them (the seas), they forgot their fish and it took its path in the sea burrowing!

18:62.t: When they moved on, he said to his ˹servant˺ boy: “To us with our lunch. Indeed, we have found great hardship in this travel of ours!”

18:63.t: He (the servant boy) said: “Did you see when we settled by the rock, I forgot the fish – none made me forget about them except Satan, lest that I should remember it – and it took its path in the sea amazingly!”

18:64.t: He (Mūsā) said: “That is what we were after!” And they went back tracing their ˹earlier˺ foot prints.

18:65.t: Then they found a servant of Ours (al-Khiḍr) to whom We bestowed mercy from Our Own and We taught him from certain Knowledge.

18:66.t: Mūsā said to him:“Shall I follow you so that you teach me of what you have been taught ˹in which there is˺ guidedness?”

18:67.t: He said: “You shall not be able to put up with me!”

18:68.t: “How could you put up with what you have no thorough knowledge of!”

18:69.t: He said: “You shall find me, by the Will of Allah, forbearing and I shall never disobey a command of yours ever!”

18:70.t: He said: “Then, should you follow me, do not ask me about a thing until I make mention of it to you!”

18:71.t: Then they set off until when they boarded the ship, he (al-Khiḍr) holed it! He (Mūsā) said: “Did you hole it!Its people might drown! You have surely come up with something macabre!”

18:72.t: He said: “Did I not say that you would not be able to put up with me!”

18:73.t: He said: “Do not find fault with me for what I have forgotten about. Do not make this affair of mine even harder for me!”

18:74.t: Then they set off until when they met a boy and he (al-Khiḍr) killed him. He (Mūsā) said: “Did you kill a pure soul not ˹even˺ for killing another! You have verily come up with a most heinous thing!

18:75.t: He said: “Did I not say to you that you would not be able to put up with me!”

18:76.t: He said: “If I ever inquire you about a thing do not accompany me! You have got your excuse from me!”

18:77.t: Then they set off until when they reached some dwellers of a village, they asked its dwellers for food but they ˹resolutely˺ denied them hospitality. Then they found a wall about to tumble down and he (al-Khiḍr) set it straight. He (Mūsā) said: “If you will, you may have asked payment for it!”

18:78.t: He said: “This is ˹then˺ a parting between me and you! I shall ˹momentarily˺ tell you the interpretation of that which you could not put up with”.

18:79.t: “As for the ship: it is owned by poor folks who venture into the sea ˹to seek provisions˺. I wanted to defect it ˹as˺ there was after them a king who would forcefully appropriate all ˹seaworthy˺ ships!”

18:80.t: “As for the boy: his parents are Believers and we feared that ˹should he live˺ he would hard press them into rebelliousness and Denial!”

18:81.t: “We wanted their Lord to replace them with ˹another˺ who is better in purity and closer in compassion!”

18:82.t: “As for the wall: it belongs to two orphan boys in the town and there is a treasure under it which belongs to them. Their father was a righteous man so your Lord wanted them to reach the height of their strength and uncover their treasure. ˹All of that is˺ A mercy from your Lord. I did not do it of my own accord. That ˹you see˺ is the interpretation of what you could not bear with!”

18:83.t: And they ask you ˹Muhammad˺ about Dhūl Qarnayn (The Two-Horned ˹King˺). Say: “I shall recite ˹some profound˺ mention about him to you!”

18:84.t: We have indeed ˹greatly˺ empowered him in the land and gave him a means for everything;

18:86.t: Until when he reached the setting place of the sun he found it setting in a turbid fount and found at it some folks. We said: “O Dhūl Qarnayn! Either you punish or dispense benevolence in their regards!

18:87.t: He said: “He who committed injustice, we shall punish. Then he is returned to his Lord and He would subject him to an unheard of Punishment!”

18:88.t: “As for he who Believes and does good deeds, his is the Most Comely ˹reward˺ and we shall say to him – of our command – ˹nothing but˺ ease!

18:90.t: until when he reached the rising place of the sun, he found it rises upon some folks for whom We made no shield between them and it!

18:91.t: Like so ˹is his affair˺, and We encompassed what he got with Knowledge!

18:93.t: until when he reached the ˹mountain˺ barriers he found, just short of them, some folks who could barely comprehend a saying!

18:94.t: They said: “O Dhūl Qarnayn! Verily Ya’jūj and Ma’jūj (Gog and Magog) ever go about sowing corruption in the land. Shall we provide you with a fee so that you may put a barrier between us and them?”

18:95.t: He said: “What my Lord has empowered me with is ˹far˺ better. ˹Only˺ Assist me with power and I shall erect a ˹fortified˺ dam between you and them!”

18:96.t: “Hand me the iron bars!” Until when he brought the two sides ˹of the mountains˺ to level, he said: “Blow!” Until when he made it (the pile of iron bars) into a flaming fire, he said: “To me with molten copper that I may pour on it!”

18:97.t: They (Ya’jūj and Ma’jūj) could not mount it ˹therein˺; they could not pierce it!

18:98.t: He said: “This is mercy from my Lord. ˹But˺ When the promise of my Lord comes to pass, He will level it (the dam) to the ground; verily the promise of my Lord is ever true!

18:99.t: And We let them on that day surge into one another, ˹then˺ the Horn is blown, and We round them all up; such a rounding up!

18:100.t: And We display Hell to the Deniers; such a display!

18:101.t: Those whose eyes where in a cover against My Reminder; ˹neither˺ were they able over hearing!

18:102.t: Might those who have Denied presume that they could take My servants as allies besides Me! Verily We have ˹carefully˺ prepared Hell as a domicile for the Deniers!

18:103.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Shall we tell you about those whose deeds are most at loss?”

18:104.t: “˹They are˺ The ones whose pursuit in the worldly life have lost their way, but they ˹still˺ think that they are doing well!”

18:105.t: These are the ones who Denied in the Signs of their Lord and in meeting Him; their deeds are made void and then We shall hold no weight for them on the Day of Judgement!

18:106.t: That ˹and˺ their requital is Hell for their Denial and taking My Signs and Messengers for mockery!

18:107.t: Verily those who have Believed and did the good deeds, theirs are ever the Gardens of Paradise as domicile.

18:108.t: Forever they abide therein, wishing not to move anywhere away from it.

18:109.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺: “Had the sea been the fountain of my Lord’s Words, it would have been depleted before the Words of my Lord are exhausted, even if We were to bring the like of it as replenishment!

18:110.t: Say ˹Muhammad˺:“I am no more than a human like yourselves, ˹but˺ to me it is revealed that your God is only the One God.Whoever hopes to meet his Lord, let him do good and Associate none in the worship of his Lord!
